# =====================================================
# FILE : 01_WORLD\ISLANDS\EAST_BLUE\EB-005_SILVER_MIST.md
# =====================================================

# ==================================================

# SILVER MIST ISLAND

# ==================================================

Sea: East Blue Classification: Canon Island World Type: One Piece
Parallel World

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# OVERVIEW

Silver Mist Island is a medium-sized island in East Blue surrounded by
dense sea fog throughout most of the year. Its isolated environment has
shaped a self-reliant fishing community and made the island a difficult
destination for pirates and merchants alike.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# GEOGRAPHY

Island Size

Medium

Approximate Diameter

72 km

Highest Elevation

Mist Peak

Terrain

-   Forests
-   Hills
-   Wetlands
-   Rocky Coast

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# CLIMATE

Cool Maritime

Dense fog is common year-round with mild summers and wet winters.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# NATURAL RESOURCES

-   Fish
-   Seaweed
-   Hardwood
-   Medicinal Herbs
-   Clay

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# MAJOR REGIONS

-   Misthaven
-   White Fog Village
-   Mistwood
-   Fogwatch Station

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SETTLEMENTS

## Misthaven

Main town and commercial center.

## White Fog Village

Fishing village.

## Mistwood Hamlet

Forestry and herbal community.

## Fogwatch Station

Marine observation post.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# HARBORS

## Misthaven Harbor

Commercial harbor.

## White Fog Pier

Fishing harbor.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# TRANSPORTATION

Travel is mainly by foot, fishing boats, and small sailing ships.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# POPULATION

Approximately 7,300 residents.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# GOVERNMENT

Locally governed by an elected mayor under World Government recognition.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# LAW ENFORCEMENT

Town Guard maintains order while Marines patrol surrounding waters.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# MARINE PRESENCE

Marine Outpost

Personnel: \~90 Marines

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# ECONOMY

Fishing, forestry, herbal gathering, and ship repair form the backbone
of the economy.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# TRADE

Exports

-   Fish
-   Sea Salt
-   Hardwood
-   Herbs

Imports

-   Steel
-   Medicine
-   Navigation Equipment

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# INDUSTRIES

-   Fishing
-   Boat Building
-   Forestry
-   Herbal Medicine

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# CRIME

Low overall.

Smuggling is the most common offense.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# BLACK MARKET

Hidden sea caves are occasionally used for illegal trade.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# PIRATE ACTIVITY

Threat Level: Low--Moderate

Small pirate crews occasionally use the surrounding fog as cover.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SECURITY LEVEL

Misthaven ★★★★☆

White Fog Village ★★★★☆

Mistwood ★★★☆☆

Sea Caves ★☆☆☆☆

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# CULTURE

Residents value cooperation, navigation skills, and respect for the sea.

Annual Mist Festival celebrates the fishing season.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# EDUCATION

Children learn navigation, fishing, reading, and mathematics.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# FOOD

Specialties

-   Silver Mist Chowder
-   Smoked Fog Mackerel
-   Seaweed Bread

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# FLORA

-   Mist Fern
-   Silver Mist Flower
-   Blue Moss

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# WILDLIFE

-   Deer
-   Rabbits
-   Wild Boars
-   Tuna
-   Sea Turtles

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# NATURAL HAZARDS

-   Dense Fog
-   Hidden Reefs
-   Storms
-   Strong Currents

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# IMPORTANT ORGANIZATIONS

-   Fishermen's Guild
-   Harbor Authority
-   Herbal Society
-   Lighthouse Service

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# IMPORTANT NPCS

-   Mayor Cedric Rowan
-   Captain Iris Vale
-   Doctor Elwin Moss
-   Rowan Pike
-   Mira Ash

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# RUMORS

1.  Strange lights appear offshore.
2.  A forgotten lighthouse hides old records.
3.  Smugglers operate beneath the northern cliffs.
4.  Rare flowers bloom deep inside Mistwood.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# RANDOM ENCOUNTERS

  Roll   Encounter
  ------ -----------------------------
  1      Fishing boat requests help
  2      Marine patrol
  3      Heavy fog
  4      Lost merchant
  5      Smugglers unloading cargo
  6      Wild boars
  7      Storm
  8      Herbalists gathering plants
  9      Drifting empty boat
  10     Damaged lighthouse beacon

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# ADVENTURE HOOKS

-   Escort merchant ships.
-   Investigate missing fishermen.
-   Stop a smuggling operation.
-   Explore Whisper Cave.
-   Recover stolen navigation charts.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# HIDDEN LOCATIONS

-   Whisper Cave
-   Forgotten Lighthouse
-   Mistwood Shrine
-   Smuggler's Cove

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SECRETS

Smugglers receive outside financial support.

Forgotten Marine records remain hidden in the old lighthouse.

Underground freshwater caves nurture rare medicinal plants.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# GM NOTES

Recommended Campaign Length

2--5 Sessions

Recommended Player Level

Beginning Characters

Core Themes

-   Exploration
-   Navigation
-   Mystery
-   Smuggling
-   Maritime Adventure


# =====================================================
# FILE : 01_WORLD\ISLANDS\EAST_BLUE\EB-006_GEKKO_ATOLL.md
# =====================================================

# ==================================================

# GEKKO ATOLL

# ==================================================

Sea: East Blue Classification: Canon Island World Type: One Piece
Parallel World

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# OVERVIEW

Gekko Atoll is a ring-shaped coral atoll in East Blue. Calm lagoons lie
inside the reef while the outer waters are notorious for hidden coral
formations that can wreck careless ships. The island supports small
coastal communities that depend on fishing, pearl diving, and coral
harvesting.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# GEOGRAPHY

Island Size

Small

Approximate Diameter

48 km

Terrain

-   Coral Reefs
-   White Sand Beaches
-   Shallow Lagoons
-   Coconut Groves

Climate

Tropical with warm temperatures year-round.

Natural Resources

-   Fish
-   Pearls
-   Coral
-   Sea Salt
-   Coconut

Major Regions

-   Coral Haven
-   Pearl Village
-   Eastern Reef
-   Blue Lagoon

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SETTLEMENTS

## Coral Haven

Main settlement and trading port.

Population

3,400

Facilities

-   Harbor
-   Market
-   Clinic
-   Marine Office
-   Boatyard

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Pearl Village

Small diving community famous for pearl harvesting.

Population

1,100

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# HARBORS

Main Harbor

Commercial and fishing vessels.

Pearl Pier

Small boats and diving crews.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# TRANSPORTATION

Travel by foot and small sailing boats.

Roads are limited.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# POPULATION

Approximately 5,200

Humans form the overwhelming majority.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# GOVERNMENT

Managed by an elected Island Council recognized by the World Government.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# LAW ENFORCEMENT

Local Harbor Guard maintains public order.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# MARINE PRESENCE

Marine Outpost

Personnel

70 Marines

Primary Duties

-   Reef Patrol
-   Anti-Smuggling
-   Merchant Escort

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# ECONOMY

Main Industries

-   Fishing
-   Pearl Diving
-   Coral Crafting
-   Boat Repair

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# TRADE

Exports

-   Pearls
-   Seafood
-   Coral Crafts
-   Sea Salt

Imports

-   Steel
-   Medicine
-   Cloth
-   Timber

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# CRIME

Overall Crime

Low

Common Crimes

-   Smuggling
-   Illegal Pearl Harvesting

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# BLACK MARKET

Hidden pearl trading occasionally occurs beyond the eastern reef.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# PIRATE ACTIVITY

Threat Level

Low

Small pirate crews occasionally raid isolated fishing boats.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SECURITY LEVEL

Coral Haven ★★★★☆

Pearl Village ★★★★☆

Outer Reef ★★☆☆☆

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# CULTURE

The people celebrate the sea and depend heavily on cooperation among
diving crews.

Annual Pearl Festival marks the end of the diving season.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# EDUCATION

Children learn swimming, navigation, and reef safety alongside basic
education.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# FOOD

Local Specialties

-   Pearl Oyster Soup
-   Grilled Reef Fish
-   Coconut Rice

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# FLORA

-   Coconut Palm
-   Sea Grass
-   Coral Flowers

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# WILDLIFE

-   Reef Fish
-   Sea Turtles
-   Dolphins
-   Rays
-   Reef Sharks

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# NATURAL HAZARDS

-   Coral Reefs
-   Tropical Storms
-   Strong Tides

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# IMPORTANT ORGANIZATIONS

-   Pearl Divers' Guild
-   Harbor Authority
-   Reef Conservation Committee

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# IMPORTANT NPCS

-   Mayor Loran Tide
-   Captain Serena Holt (Marine)
-   Darius Reef (Master Diver)
-   Mira Shell (Guild Leader)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# RUMORS

1.  A giant pearl lies hidden beneath the Blue Lagoon.
2.  Smugglers use underwater caves beneath the reef.
3.  Divers have found ancient ruins below the lagoon.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# RANDOM ENCOUNTERS

  Roll   Encounter
  ------ ---------------------------------
  1      Pearl divers request assistance
  2      Marine reef patrol
  3      Sea turtle nest
  4      Merchant ship aground
  5      Hidden cave entrance
  6      Storm approaches
  7      Illegal pearl hunters
  8      Playful dolphins
  9      Damaged lighthouse buoy
  10     Abandoned skiff

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# ADVENTURE HOOKS

-   Recover stolen pearls.
-   Explore submerged ruins.
-   Stop illegal coral harvesting.
-   Escort a merchant convoy.
-   Rescue missing divers.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# HIDDEN LOCATIONS

-   Blue Lagoon Cavern
-   Sunken Shrine
-   Smuggler's Tunnel
-   Western Coral Arch

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SECRETS

A forgotten underwater shrine predates the current settlements.

Some merchants secretly finance illegal pearl harvesting.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# GM NOTES

Recommended Campaign Length

2--4 Sessions

Recommended Player Level

Beginning Characters

Core Themes

-   Exploration
-   Diving
-   Maritime Adventure
-   Trade
-   Mystery

# ==================================================

# SYRUP VILLAGE

# ==================================================

Sea: East Blue Classification: Canon Island World Type: One Piece
Parallel World

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# OVERVIEW

Syrup Village is a peaceful rural settlement in East Blue surrounded by
fertile farmland and gentle hills. It is known for its quiet lifestyle,
skilled craftsmen, and agricultural production. Although relatively
safe, merchant traffic occasionally attracts thieves and pirates.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# GEOGRAPHY

Island Size

Small

Terrain

-   Rolling Hills
-   Forests
-   Farmland
-   Sandy Coast

Climate

Warm Temperate

Natural Resources

-   Timber
-   Wheat
-   Fruit
-   Fresh Water
-   Livestock

Major Areas

-   Syrup Village
-   North Farm
-   Eastern Forest
-   Coastal Cliffs

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SETTLEMENTS

## Syrup Village

Population

4,800

Facilities

-   Town Hall
-   Clinic
-   Marketplace
-   Ship Dock
-   Workshop District

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## North Farm

Agricultural community supplying crops and livestock.

Population

900

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# TRANSPORTATION

Travel by foot, wagon, and small sailing vessels.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# POPULATION

5,700

Low

Low

itself.

# NATURAL HAZARDS

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# IMPORTANT NPCS

-   Chief Edwin Hart
-   Captain Naomi Cross (Marine)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# RUMORS

1.  Strange sounds come from the old forest at night.
2.  A forgotten mansion hides valuable documents.
3.  Smugglers use abandoned storage sheds near the coast.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

  Roll   Encounter
  ------ ----------------------

# =====================================================
# FILE : 01_WORLD\ISLANDS\EAST_BLUE\EB-008_BARATIE.md
# =====================================================

# ==================================================

# BARATIE

# ==================================================

Sea: East Blue Classification: Canon Sea Restaurant World Type: One
Piece Parallel World

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# OVERVIEW

Baratie is a floating sea restaurant operating in East Blue. Built upon
a massive ship, it welcomes travelers, merchants, Marines, bounty
hunters, and pirates alike. Neutrality and hospitality are valued above
politics, though violence against guests is met with swift response.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# STRUCTURE

Main Sections

-   Grand Dining Hall
-   Guest Cabins
-   Central Kitchen
-   Supply Warehouse
-   Observation Deck
-   Crew Quarters
-   Dry Storage
-   Docking Platforms

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# OPERATIONS

Primary Services

-   Restaurant
-   Lodging
-   Ship Resupply
-   Fresh Water
-   Emergency Repairs

Average Daily Visitors

120--250

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# POPULATION

Permanent Residents

Approximately 140

Most are chefs, servers, suppliers, and sailors.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# MANAGEMENT

Owned and managed by an independent hospitality association recognized
by the World Government.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SECURITY

Internal Security Team

Personnel

25

Responsibilities

-   Protect Guests
-   Prevent Theft
-   Break Up Fights
-   Coordinate with Marines

Nearby Marine visits are frequent but Baratie remains independent.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# ECONOMY

Income Sources

-   Dining
-   Lodging
-   Ship Supplies
-   Catering

Major Purchases

-   Seafood
-   Meat
-   Vegetables
-   Spices
-   Fuel

Excellent cooking and respectful service are considered matters of

shipments.

A hidden compartment contains records of influential visitors from
across East Blue.

1--3 Sessions

Recommended Player Level

Beginning Characters

# =====================================================
# FILE : 01_WORLD\ISLANDS\EAST_BLUE\EB-009_BLACKTIDE_KEY.md
# =====================================================

# ==================================================

# BLACKTIDE KEY

# ==================================================

Sea: East Blue Classification: Original Island World Type: One Piece
Parallel World

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# OVERVIEW

Blacktide Key is a rugged island on the eastern trade routes of East
Blue. Dark volcanic rock gives its shoreline a black appearance, while
dangerous currents discourage inexperienced sailors. The island survives
through mining, fishing, and servicing passing ships.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# GEOGRAPHY

Island Size

Medium

Terrain

-   Black Cliffs
-   Rocky Beaches
-   Volcanic Hills
-   Sparse Forests

Climate

Warm Coastal

Natural Resources

-   Basalt
-   Iron Ore
-   Coal
-   Fish
-   Fresh Water

Major Regions

-   Black Harbor
-   Ash Ridge
-   Iron Valley
-   Tidewatch Point

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SETTLEMENTS

## Black Harbor

Population

4,200

The island's commercial center with docks, markets, and workshops.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Iron Valley

Population

1,300

Mining settlement supplying ore and stone.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Tidewatch Point

Small Marine lookout overlooking the eastern sea.

Population

250

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# HARBORS

Black Harbor serves merchant ships, fishing vessels, and Marine patrol
boats.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# TRANSPORTATION

Primary travel uses coastal roads, wagons, and small sailing ships.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# POPULATION

Estimated Population

5,900

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# GOVERNMENT

Led by an elected Harbor Governor supported by local council members.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# LAW ENFORCEMENT

Harbor Guard maintains order throughout the settlements.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# MARINE PRESENCE

Marine Branch

Personnel

110 Marines

Responsibilities

-   Merchant Protection
-   Pirate Suppression
-   Coastal Patrols

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# ECONOMY

-   Fishing
-   Stone Cutting
-   Ship Repair

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# TRADE

Exports

-   Iron Ore
-   Basalt
-   Coal
-   Fish

Imports

-   Medicine
-   Lumber
-   Steel Tools

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# CRIME

Overall Crime

Moderate

Common Crimes

-   Smuggling
-   Cargo Theft
-   Illegal Mining

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# BLACK MARKET

Several abandoned mine tunnels are used by criminal groups to hide
stolen goods.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# PIRATE ACTIVITY

Threat Level

Moderate

Pirates frequently ambush merchant ships near the surrounding reefs.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SECURITY LEVEL

Iron Valley ★★★☆☆

Outer Coast ★★☆☆☆

Hidden Mines ★☆☆☆☆

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Hard work and craftsmanship define daily life. Annual mining
competitions and harbor festivals attract visitors from nearby islands.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# EDUCATION

Schools emphasize mathematics, navigation, engineering, and mining
safety.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# FOOD

Local Specialties

-   Charcoal Grilled Tuna
-   Black Stone Bread
-   Seafood Stew

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# FLORA

-   Iron Pine
-   Salt Grass
-   Wild Sage

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# WILDLIFE

-   Mountain Goats
-   Seabirds
-   Wild Boars
-   Tuna
-   Reef Sharks

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# NATURAL HAZARDS

-   Falling Rocks
-   Rough Seas
-   Coastal Storms
-   Mine Collapses

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# IMPORTANT ORGANIZATIONS

-   Miners' Guild
-   Harbor Authority
-   Shipwright Cooperative

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# IMPORTANT NPCS

-   Commander Elena Ward
-   Guildmaster Ronan Steel
-   Chief Engineer Mira Flint

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# RUMORS

1.  A forgotten mine contains valuable relics.
2.  Smugglers use old tunnels beneath Black Harbor.
3.  A pirate captain seeks a hidden cache nearby.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

  ------ -------------------------

-   Stop illegal ore smuggling.

-   Explore an abandoned mine.

# =====================================================
# FILE : 01_WORLD\ISLANDS\EAST_BLUE\EB-010_ARLONG_PARK.md
# =====================================================

# ==================================================

# ARLONG PARK

# ==================================================

Sea: East Blue Classification: Canon Location World Type: One Piece
Parallel World

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# OVERVIEW

Arlong Park was once the headquarters of the Arlong Pirates. In the
Parallel World, the site has been reclaimed after the crew's fall. The
remaining structures stand as a reminder of the island's troubled past,
while nearby communities work to rebuild the area into a peaceful
coastal district.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# GEOGRAPHY

Island Size

Small Coastal District

Terrain

-   Coastal Plain
-   Stone Complex
-   Beaches
-   Mangrove Wetlands

Climate

Tropical Maritime

Natural Resources

-   Fish
-   Coral
-   Fresh Water
-   Timber

Major Areas

-   Former Arlong Park
-   Reclaimed Harbor
-   Fisher's Village
-   Mangrove Coast

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SETTLEMENTS

## Reclaimed Harbor

Population

2,900

The primary civilian settlement built after the liberation of the area.

Facilities

-   Harbor
-   Market
-   Clinic
-   Warehouse
-   Marine Office

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Fisher's Village

Population

1,200

A quiet fishing community supplying seafood across East Blue.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# HARBORS

The restored harbor supports fishing vessels and small merchant ships.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# TRANSPORTATION

Travel is primarily by foot and boat.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# POPULATION

Estimated Population

4,300

------------------------------------------------------------------------

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# LAW ENFORCEMENT

groups from returning.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Personnel

120 Marines

-   Civil Protection

-   Ship Repair

Low

-   Coconut Palms

# WILDLIFE

-   Reef Fish

2.  Secret passages connect the underground chambers to the sea.
3.  Someone has begun buying old Arlong Pirate artifacts.

  ------ --------------------------

# FILE : 01_WORLD\ISLANDS\EAST_BLUE\EB-011_MOONSHELL_ISLAND.md
# =====================================================

# ==================================================

# MOONSHELL ISLAND

# ==================================================

Sea: East Blue Classification: Original Island World Type: One Piece
Parallel World

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# OVERVIEW

Moonshell Island is a tranquil island famous for its brilliant white
beaches and rare moon-colored shells that wash ashore during certain
tides. The island thrives on fishing, shellcraft, and coastal trade
while remaining relatively untouched by large-scale conflict.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# GEOGRAPHY

Island Size

Small

Terrain

-   White Sand Beaches
-   Coconut Forests
-   Limestone Hills
-   Calm Bays

Climate

Tropical Maritime

Natural Resources

-   Moonshells
-   Fish
-   Limestone
-   Fresh Water
-   Coconut

Major Areas

-   Moon Bay
-   Shell Village
-   Crescent Cliffs
-   Whisper Grove

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SETTLEMENTS

## Shell Village

Population

3,100

Main settlement featuring markets, workshops, a clinic, and harbor.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Moon Bay

Population

900

Fishing community known for collecting rare shells.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# HARBORS

Moon Harbor supports fishing boats, merchant ships, and Marine patrol
craft.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# TRANSPORTATION

Travel is mainly by foot, horse carts, and coastal sailing boats.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# POPULATION

Estimated Population

4,600

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Low

2.  Smugglers are purchasing rare Moonshells in secret.

# =====================================================
# FILE : 01_WORLD\ISLANDS\EAST_BLUE\EB-012_LOGUETOWN.md
# =====================================================

# ==================================================
# LOGUETOWN
# ==================================================

Sea: East Blue
Classification: Canon Island
World Type: One Piece Parallel World

---

# OVERVIEW

Loguetown is the largest commercial city in East Blue and serves as the primary gateway to Reverse Mountain and the Grand Line. Nearly every captain sailing toward the Grand Line eventually visits Loguetown to resupply, recruit crew members, repair ships, and prepare for the dangerous voyage ahead.

Because of its strategic location, Loguetown maintains one of East Blue's strongest Marine presences. Merchants, bounty hunters, pirates, craftsmen, scholars, and travelers from every Blue can be found within its busy streets.

Although considered one of the safest major cities in East Blue, criminal organizations constantly attempt to exploit its enormous flow of commerce.

---

# GEOGRAPHY

Island Size

Large

Approximate Diameter

145 km

Travel Time by Foot

3–4 Days

Highest Elevation

Execution Hill

Terrain Distribution

30% Urban

25% Forest

20% Plains

15% Hills

10% Coastline

---

# CLIMATE

Climate Type

Warm Maritime

Spring

Comfortable temperatures with occasional rain.

Summer

Hot and humid.

Autumn

Strong sea winds.

Ideal sailing conditions.

Winter

Cool but rarely cold.

---

# NATURAL RESOURCES

Primary Resources

- Fresh Water
- Hardwood
- Fish
- Clay
- Limestone

Rare Resources

- High-quality Ship Timber
- Navigation Minerals

---

# MAJOR REGIONS

## Central City

Political and commercial center.

---

## Harbor District

Merchant docks, warehouses and shipyards.

---

## Market Quarter

Largest marketplace in East Blue.

---

## Marine District

Marine Headquarters and military facilities.

---

## Old Town

Historic residential district.

---

## Execution Hill

Historic landmark overlooking the city.

---

# SETTLEMENTS

Loguetown is the largest urban settlement in East Blue. Unlike most islands that revolve around a single village, the city is divided into specialized districts connected by wide roads, canals, and public plazas.

---

## Central City

Type

Capital District

Population

Approximately 31,000

Description

The administrative heart of Loguetown. Government offices, banks, public institutions, and large merchant companies operate here.

Major Facilities

- City Hall
- Central Plaza
- Public Library
- General Hospital
- Banking House
- Commercial Court
- Grand Hotel

Primary Occupations

- Government Officials
- Merchants
- Lawyers
- Bankers
- Doctors
- Scholars

---

## Harbor District

Type

Commercial Port

Population

Approximately 12,000

Description

The busiest harbor in East Blue. Hundreds of vessels enter and leave each week carrying cargo destined for every Blue and the Grand Line.

Major Facilities

- Cargo Docks
- Dry Dock
- Customs Office
- Shipyards
- Warehouses
- Fish Market
- Marine Inspection Pier

Primary Occupations

- Sailors
- Dock Workers
- Shipwrights
- Merchants
- Warehouse Workers

---

## Market Quarter

Type

Commercial District

Population

Approximately 8,500

Description

An enormous marketplace selling nearly every legal product available within East Blue.

Popular Businesses

- Weapon Shops
- Clothing Stores
- Bookstores
- Restaurants
- Medical Suppliers
- Navigation Equipment
- Blacksmiths

---

## Old Town

Type

Residential District

Population

Approximately 11,000

Description

The oldest neighborhood in Loguetown.

Stone streets, historical buildings and family-owned businesses preserve the city's early history.

---

## Marine District

Type

Military Zone

Population

Approximately 2,800

Description

Restricted military district containing the city's Marine headquarters and training facilities.

Civilian access is limited.

---

# HARBORS

Loguetown possesses the busiest harbor network in East Blue.

---

## Grand Commercial Harbor

Purpose

International Trade

Facilities

- Dry Dock
- Cargo Crane
- Customs Office
- Passenger Terminal
- Marine Inspection

Average Daily Ships

80–150

---

## Eastern Fishing Port

Purpose

Fishing Fleet

Average Boats

160

Exports

- Fresh Fish
- Shellfish
- Seaweed

---

## Marine Naval Harbor

Purpose

Military Operations

Facilities

- Battleship Docks
- Supply Depot
- Armory
- Fuel Storage
- Repair Dock

Restricted Area

Authorized Personnel Only

---

# ROAD NETWORK

Road Quality

Excellent

Major Roads

Grand Harbor Road

Connects the harbor with Central City.

Merchant Avenue

Primary commercial road.

Marine Boulevard

Military access road.

Old Stone Road

Historic district route.

---

# TRANSPORTATION

Common Methods

- Walking
- Horse Cart
- Passenger Carriage
- River Ferry
- Merchant Ship
- Sailing Ship

Travel Time

Harbor → Central City

20 Minutes

Central City → Marine District

30 Minutes

Central City → Old Town

15 Minutes

Market Quarter → Harbor

10 Minutes

---

# POPULATION

Estimated Population

Approximately 65,000

Human

95%

Fish-Man

2%

Mink

Less than 1%

Longarm Tribe

Rare

Longleg Tribe

Rare

Visitors

Thousands arrive every week.

Population Distribution

Urban

92%

Rural

8%

Average Literacy

Very High

Average Wealth

High

Crime Rate

Moderate

---

# GOVERNMENT

## Political System

Loguetown is administered by a civilian city government under the authority of the World Government.

Daily administration is managed by the Mayor and City Council while Marine authorities retain jurisdiction over piracy, international crime, and maritime security.

Departments

- Commerce
- Infrastructure
- Finance
- Public Health
- Harbor Administration
- Public Safety
- Education

---

# LAW ENFORCEMENT

## City Guard

The City Guard maintains law and order within civilian districts.

Responsibilities

- Patrol Streets
- Investigate Crimes
- Traffic Control
- Public Safety
- Festival Security

The City Guard cooperates closely with Marine investigators during major criminal cases.

---

# MARINE PRESENCE

## Loguetown Marine Base

Classification

Regional Headquarters

Personnel

Approximately 1,200 Marines

Facilities

- Headquarters
- Officer Academy
- Barracks
- Military Hospital
- Prison
- Armory
- Supply Depot
- Cannon Battery
- Naval Dockyard

---

## Standard Fleet

Harbor Patrol Boats

12

Medium Warships

8

Large Battleships

3

Transport Ships

5

Supply Ships

6

---

## Marine Responsibilities

- Suppress Piracy
- Protect Merchant Shipping
- Capture Wanted Criminals
- Escort Government Officials
- Defend Reverse Mountain Routes
- Coordinate Emergency Response

---

## Cooperation

The Marines regularly cooperate with

- City Guard
- Harbor Authority
- Merchant Guild
- Medical Association
- Customs Office

Joint operations are common due to the city's strategic importance.

---

# ECONOMY

## Economic Profile

Loguetown possesses the largest economy in East Blue.

Its strategic location before Reverse Mountain allows it to serve as the final resupply point for ships preparing to enter the Grand Line.

Economic Stability

★★★★★

Currency

Belly

Employment

Very High

Primary Economic Drivers

- Maritime Trade
- Shipbuilding
- Tourism
- Logistics
- Fishing
- Government Services

---

# MAJOR INDUSTRIES

## Maritime Trade

The city's largest industry.

Thousands of tons of cargo pass through the harbor every month.

Common Goods

- Food
- Lumber
- Steel
- Textiles
- Medicine
- Navigation Equipment

---

## Shipbuilding

Loguetown maintains dozens of private shipyards.

Ships Produced

- Fishing Boats
- Merchant Ships
- Passenger Ships
- Patrol Boats

Services

- Hull Repair
- Sail Replacement
- Cannon Installation
- Navigation Equipment

---

## Fishing

Fishing fleets operate daily throughout East Blue.

Common Catch

- Tuna
- Sardines
- Swordfish
- Shellfish
- Seaweed

---

## Tourism

Many travelers visit Loguetown because of its historical importance.

Popular Attractions

- Execution Hill
- Harbor District
- Grand Market
- Historic Streets
- Marine Museum

---

## Manufacturing

Numerous workshops produce

- Weapons
- Furniture
- Barrels
- Rope
- Clothing
- Glassware

---

# TRADE

## Regional Trade Network

Loguetown connects East Blue with every other major sea through merchant fleets.

Ships regularly depart toward

- Shells Town
- Orange Town
- Syrup Village
- Baratie
- Conomi Region

International merchants preparing for the Grand Line also purchase supplies here.

---

## Major Exports

Industrial Goods

- Steel Tools
- Weapons
- Furniture
- Ship Parts

Food Products

- Salted Fish
- Flour
- Dried Meat
- Fresh Fruit

Commercial Goods

- Navigation Equipment
- Maps
- Medical Supplies

---

## Major Imports

Luxury Goods

- Wine
- Jewelry
- Silk

Industrial Materials

- High-grade Steel
- Coal
- Rare Timber

Medical Goods

- Imported Herbs
- Surgical Equipment
- Chemicals

---

# MERCHANT GUILD

The Merchant Guild coordinates commercial activities throughout the city.

Responsibilities

- Trade Licenses
- Commercial Arbitration
- Warehouse Management
- Shipping Contracts
- Merchant Convoys

Influence

★★★★★

---

# BANKING

Loguetown possesses East Blue's largest banking district.

Services

- Currency Exchange
- Merchant Loans
- Trade Financing
- Safe Deposits
- Letters of Credit

Large commercial transactions are almost entirely paper-based rather than cash.

---

# CRIME

Overall Crime Level

Moderate

Although heavily protected by Marines, the enormous population attracts organized crime.

Common Crimes

- Pickpocketing
- Counterfeit Currency
- Cargo Theft
- Smuggling
- Illegal Gambling
- Fraud

Violent crime remains relatively uncommon within the city center.

---

# ORGANIZED CRIME

Several criminal groups secretly compete for influence over harbor commerce.

Activities include

- Smuggling
- Money Laundering
- Illegal Auctions
- Forged Documents
- Black-market Shipping

Most organizations avoid direct confrontation with Marines.

---

# BLACK MARKET

Despite strict Marine enforcement, an underground market continues to operate within hidden warehouses.

Common Contraband

- Illegal Weapons
- Stolen Cargo
- Forged Identification
- Counterfeit Belly
- Smuggled Medicine

Transactions usually occur at night through intermediaries.

---

# PIRATE ACTIVITY

Pirate Threat Level

High Outside Harbor

Low Inside Harbor

Many pirate crews visit Loguetown to purchase supplies before entering the Grand Line.

Most avoid causing trouble inside the city due to the overwhelming Marine presence.

Typical Crew Size

10–40 Pirates

Average Bounty

8,000,000–25,000,000 Belly

Devil Fruit users remain uncommon but appear more frequently than elsewhere in East Blue.

---

## Common Pirate Activities

- Purchasing Supplies
- Recruiting Crew Members
- Selling Loot
- Gathering Intelligence
- Secret Meetings
- Smuggling

---

# SECURITY LEVEL

Marine Headquarters

★★★★★

Central City

★★★★★

Market Quarter

★★★★☆

Harbor District

★★★★☆

Old Town

★★★☆☆

Outer Warehouses

★★★☆☆

Hidden Alleys

★★☆☆☆

Abandoned Dockyards

★★☆☆☆
---

# CULTURE

## General Culture

Loguetown is often called **"The City of Beginnings and Endings."**

For many people, it is the final destination of a peaceful life.

For adventurers, pirates, Marines, and merchants, it is the first step toward the Grand Line.

Because thousands of travelers arrive every month, the city has developed one of the most diverse cultures in East Blue.

Residents are accustomed to meeting people from every sea and generally judge others by their actions rather than their origins.

---

## Hospitality

Visitors are welcomed regardless of occupation, provided they obey local laws.

Restaurants, inns, taverns, and bathhouses remain open late into the night due to the constant flow of travelers.

---

## Social Classes

### Government Officials

Responsible for administration and public services.

---

### Merchants

One of the wealthiest and most influential groups.

Many own shipping companies operating throughout East Blue.

---

### Craftsmen

Highly respected.

Especially

- Shipwrights
- Blacksmiths
- Navigators
- Cartographers

---

### Sailors

Merchant sailors form a large portion of the city's workforce.

---

### Dock Workers

Responsible for loading and unloading cargo around the clock.

---

# EDUCATION

Public education is available throughout the city.

Core Subjects

- Reading
- Writing
- Mathematics
- Geography
- Navigation
- Maritime Law
- History
- Commerce

Private academies also offer advanced education for navigation, engineering, and finance.

---

# RELIGION

No official religion exists.

Many residents instead observe traditional ceremonies honoring

- The Sea
- Ancestors
- Safe Voyages

Small shrines are scattered throughout the city.

---

# FESTIVALS

## Founder's Festival

Celebrates the establishment of Loguetown.

Activities

- Fireworks
- Music
- Merchant Fair
- Street Performances

---

## Harbor Festival

Held each summer.

Includes

- Ship Parade
- Sailing Competition
- Seafood Market
- Navigation Contest

---

## Voyage Blessing Day

Before the beginning of the major sailing season, priests, navigators, and harbor officials bless departing ships.

---

# FOOD

Common Meals

Breakfast

- Bread
- Eggs
- Fish Soup

Lunch

- Rice
- Seafood
- Vegetables

Dinner

- Steak
- Roasted Fish
- Seasonal Produce

---

## Local Specialties

Grand Line Seafood Stew

Prepared using seafood imported from every Blue.

---

Harbor Steak

Large grilled beef steak served with herb butter.

---

Navigator's Pie

Fruit pie commonly purchased by sailors before long voyages.

---

# FLORA

Common Trees

- Oak
- Pine
- Cedar
- Maple

Medicinal Plants

- Kingsleaf
- Blue Mint
- Silver Fern

Rare Plants

- Wind Blossom
- Harbor Orchid

---

# WILDLIFE

Harmless Animals

- Deer
- Rabbits
- Foxes
- Seabirds

Marine Life

- Tuna
- Swordfish
- Flying Fish
- Dolphins

Dangerous Animals

Occasionally sharks approach the outer harbor.

Sea Kings are extremely rare.

---

# NATURAL HAZARDS

Possible environmental dangers

- Tropical Storms
- High Waves
- Harbor Fires
- Flooding
- Strong Sea Winds

Emergency evacuation procedures are practiced regularly.

---

# IMPORTANT ORGANIZATIONS

## Merchant Guild

Influence

★★★★★

Coordinates trade throughout East Blue.

---

## Harbor Authority

Influence

★★★★★

Maintains docks, shipping lanes and customs.

---

## Navigation Academy

Influence

★★★★☆

Trains navigators preparing for the Grand Line.

---

## Medical Association

Influence

★★★★☆

Operates hospitals and quarantine facilities.

---

## Shipwright Union

Influence

★★★★☆

Maintains construction standards for commercial vessels.

---

# IMPORTANT NPCS

All characters below are original to the Parallel World.

---

## Mayor Adrian Whitlock

Occupation

Mayor of Loguetown

Age

56

Personality

Calm

Diplomatic

Experienced

Description

A veteran administrator respected for balancing commerce, public safety, and relations with the Marines. He believes Loguetown's greatest strength is its openness to the world.

---

## Commodore Marcus Hale

Occupation

Marine Base Commander

Age

47

Justice

Balanced Justice

Description

Commander of the Loguetown Marine Headquarters.

An experienced strategist who prioritizes civilian safety and rapid responses to pirate activity.

Strengths

- Leadership
- Naval Strategy
- Investigation

Weakness

Often overworks himself.

---

## Helena Cross

Occupation

Merchant Guild Master

Age

41

Description

Controls one of the largest merchant networks in East Blue.

Known for fair negotiations and maintaining trade stability.

---

## Doctor Evelyn Ross

Occupation

Chief Physician

Age

45

Specialty

Emergency Medicine

Maritime Diseases

Description

Director of Loguetown General Hospital.

Frequently coordinates medical aid for incoming ships.

---

## Victor Stone

Occupation

Master Shipwright

Age

58

Description

One of East Blue's finest shipbuilders.

His docks are always filled with merchant vessels preparing for the Grand Line.

---

## Professor Lionel Graves

Occupation

Head Instructor

Navigation Academy

Age

61

Description

A legendary navigator who now trains the next generation of sailors.

His lectures attract students from every Blue.

---

# RUMORS

Game Masters may introduce these naturally.

1. A merchant recently purchased an ancient sea chart for an enormous fortune.

2. Strange lights have been seen beneath the abandoned eastern docks.

3. Someone has been forging Marine documents.

4. Several cargo ships disappeared shortly after leaving Loguetown.

5. A hidden warehouse secretly stores stolen Grand Line goods.

6. Pirates are searching for an unknown artifact hidden somewhere in the city.

7. An underground tunnel supposedly connects the harbor to Old Town.

8. A wealthy collector quietly purchases historical relics every month.

9. A mysterious navigator predicts storms with impossible accuracy.

10. An abandoned lighthouse still shines on moonless nights.

---

# RANDOM ENCOUNTERS

Roll 1D10 while exploring the city.

| Roll | Encounter |
|------|-----------|
|1|Marine patrol requests identification.|
|2|Merchant offers temporary work.|
|3|Street performer gathers a large crowd.|
|4|Lost child asks for help.|
|5|Cargo accident blocks the street.|
|6|Bounty hunters question local witnesses.|
|7|Heavy rain floods nearby roads.|
|8|Traveling doctor treats injured sailors.|
|9|Warehouse workers discover suspicious cargo.|
|10|A cloaked stranger quietly follows the party.|

---

# ADVENTURE HOOKS

Escort an important merchant convoy preparing to enter the Grand Line.

Investigate counterfeit Belly coins circulating throughout the markets.

Discover who is financing an expanding smuggling network.

Search abandoned warehouses for missing government cargo.

Protect a famous navigator targeted by pirates.

Investigate strange disappearances around the eastern docks.

Recover confidential Marine documents before they reach criminals.

Locate an underground tunnel used by organized crime.

Stop illegal weapon shipments hidden among merchant cargo.

Uncover the truth behind a forgotten historical landmark.

---

# HIDDEN LOCATIONS

## Forgotten Warehouse

An abandoned warehouse beneath the Harbor District.

Contains sealed cargo dating back decades.

---

## Underground Smuggling Tunnel

Secret passage connecting Old Town with hidden harbor caves.

Still partially operational.

---

## Lighthouse Archive

A forgotten archive beneath the city's oldest lighthouse.

Contains navigation records more than one hundred years old.

---

## Hidden Naval Bunker

An emergency military facility constructed beneath the Marine Headquarters.

Only senior officers know of its existence.

---

# SECRETS

Several respected merchants secretly cooperate with smugglers to avoid customs inspections.

A forgotten tunnel beneath Loguetown predates the city's modern construction.

One senior government official has accepted bribes from organized crime for years.

Ancient navigation records hidden beneath the lighthouse describe sea routes that no longer appear on modern maps.

A criminal syndicate plans to establish a permanent black-market auction inside the Harbor District.

---

# GM NOTES

Recommended Campaign Length

5–10 Sessions

Recommended Player Level

Beginning to Intermediate Characters

Core Themes

- Maritime Adventure
- Trade
- Justice
- Crime Investigation
- Politics
- Exploration

## GM Guidance

Loguetown should feel like a living metropolis where hundreds of independent stories unfold every day.

Merchant prices, Marine patrols, public opinion, criminal activity, and trade volume should evolve naturally in response to player actions.

Because it is the final major stop before the Grand Line, Loguetown is an excellent location for:

- Recruiting crew members
- Purchasing equipment
- Gathering intelligence
- Introducing rival crews
- Beginning long-term story arcs
- Establishing recurring NPCs

Player choices made here can have lasting consequences that echo throughout the rest of the campaign.

# END OF FILE

# =====================================================

# EAST BLUE ECONOMY

## Main Exports
- Fish
- Fruit
- Timber
- Grain
- Salt
- Iron

## Imports
- Medicine
- Luxury Goods
- Navigation Equipment
- Weapons

## Currency
Standard Belly economy.

## Trade
Merchant shipping is frequent between major islands.


# =====================================================
# FILE : 01_WORLD\ISLANDS\EAST_BLUE\HISTORY.md
# =====================================================

# EAST BLUE HISTORY

## Parallel World Rule

Canon geography is preserved.
All historical figures are original characters.

---

## Early Era

Fishing settlements gradually expanded into trading ports.

---

## Trade Expansion

Merchant routes linked the major islands and encouraged Marine deployment.

---

## Age of Piracy

Local pirate crews emerged, targeting merchant shipping rather than conquering territory.

---

## Modern Era

East Blue is considered peaceful compared to the Grand Line but still experiences piracy, corruption, smuggling and political conflicts.

---

## GM Usage

History should influence politics, local traditions and rumors without forcing player outcomes.


# =====================================================
# FILE : 01_WORLD\ISLANDS\EAST_BLUE\MARINE.md
# =====================================================

# EAST BLUE MARINES

## Purpose
The East Blue Marine Branch is responsible for maintaining order, suppressing piracy, protecting merchant traffic, enforcing World Government law, and responding to emergencies.

---

## Organization

### Regional Headquarters
Coordinates operations across East Blue.

### Local Bases
Located near major ports and populated islands.

### Outposts
Small stations responsible for nearby villages and shipping lanes.

---

## Responsibilities

- Patrol sea lanes
- Escort merchant vessels
- Investigate crimes
- Arrest pirates and bandits
- Disaster relief
- Protect World Government facilities

---

## Patrol Levels

| Level | Description |
|---|---|
| I | Harbor Patrol |
| II | Coastal Patrol |
| III | Open Sea Patrol |
| IV | Anti-Pirate Operation |
| V | Emergency Mobilization |

---

## Standard Fleet

### Patrol Boat
Crew: 10–30

### Medium Warship
Crew: 50–120

### Large Battleship
Crew: 150+

---

## Response Priority

1. Civilian distress
2. Pirate attack
3. Marine base emergency
4. Government request
5. Routine patrol

---

## Investigation Procedure

1. Receive report
2. Verify evidence
3. Dispatch patrol
4. Capture suspects
5. Submit report
6. Update bounty if necessary

---

## Relationships

### Civilians
Generally positive.

### Merchants
Strong cooperation.

### Pirates
Hostile.

---

## Corruption

Although Marine doctrine values justice, individual officers may abuse authority. Such corruption should remain local and become story hooks rather than the norm.

---

## GM Guidelines

Use Marines as an active organization rather than stationary NPCs.

- Patrols move between islands.
- Reports spread through Den Den Mushi.
- Pirate actions increase patrol frequency.
- Helping Marines improves reputation.


# =====================================================
# FILE : 01_WORLD\ISLANDS\EAST_BLUE\PIRATES.md
# =====================================================

# EAST BLUE PIRATES

## Overview
Most pirate crews are inexperienced and operate locally.

## Typical Crew
- Crew Size: 5–30
- Average Bounty: 3–20 Million
- Devil Fruit Users: Rare
- Haki Users: Extremely Rare

## Activities
- Cargo theft
- Smuggling
- Port extortion
- Kidnapping
- Treasure hunting

## GM Notes
East Blue pirates should feel dangerous to civilians but not world-level threats.


# =====================================================
# FILE : 01_WORLD\ISLANDS\EAST_BLUE\ROUTES.md
# =====================================================

# EAST BLUE ROUTES

## Major Civilian Route
Dawn Island → Shells Town → Orange Town → Syrup Village → Baratie → Loguetown

## Marine Routes
Frequent patrol loops connect Marine bases.

## Dangerous Areas
- Hidden reefs
- Pirate ambush zones
- Seasonal fog


# =====================================================
# FILE : 01_WORLD\ISLANDS\EAST_BLUE\RUMORS.md
# =====================================================

# EAST BLUE RUMORS

Possible rumors for Game Masters:

1. A ghost ship has appeared offshore.
2. Pirates seek an old treasure map.
3. A Marine officer is accepting bribes.
4. A Devil Fruit washed onto a remote beach.
5. Sea Kings have been spotted unusually close to shore.
6. A merchant convoy disappeared without a trace.


# =====================================================
# FILE : 01_WORLD\ISLANDS\EAST_BLUE\SEA_INFORMATION.md
# =====================================================

# EAST BLUE SEA INFORMATION

## Overview
East Blue is the calmest of the Four Blues. Navigation is simple, weather is stable, piracy exists but is generally limited to small crews, and Marine influence is strong.

## Sea Characteristics
- Navigation Difficulty: Low
- Weather Stability: High
- Sea King Activity: Rare
- Average Wave Height: 1–5 m
- Visibility: Excellent

## Navigation
Uses compass, stars, sea charts and coastline. Log Pose is unnecessary.

## Common Wildlife
- Tuna
- Marlin
- Flying Fish
- Dolphins
- Sea Turtles
- Sharks

## Strategic Role
East Blue supplies food, timber, fish and recruits to the World Government and Marine forces.

## GM Notes
Ideal starting sea for beginner campaigns focused on exploration, local piracy and character growth.

# PURPOSE

ไฟล์นี้กำหนดมาตรฐานในการสร้างเกาะใหม่ (Original Island)

เกาะใหม่ทุกเกาะต้องให้ความรู้สึกว่าเป็นส่วนหนึ่งของจักรวาล One Piece

ห้ามสร้างเกาะที่ขัดกับกฎของโลก

ทุกเกาะต้องมีเหตุผลในการดำรงอยู่

☐ มีเหตุผลในการดำรงอยู่

☐ มีความขัดแย้ง

☐ มี Quest

☐ มีความลับ

☐ มีผลกระทบต่อโลก

☐ ผู้เล่นสามารถเปลี่ยนแปลงเกาะได้

---

# PARALLEL WORLD RULE

PROJECT ASTER ENGINE

ใช้เกาะ Canon เป็นแกนหลัก

และเพิ่ม Original Islands เข้าไปทั่วโลก

สัดส่วนแนะนำ

- Canon Islands : 70%

- Original Islands : 30%

เกาะ Original ต้องสามารถเชื่อมต่อกับภูมิศาสตร์ของ One Piece ได้อย่างสมเหตุสมผล

---

END OF FILE

# FILE : 01_WORLD\ISLANDS\ISLAND_TEMPLATE.md
# =====================================================

# ISLAND_TEMPLATE.md

> PROJECT ASTER ENGINE
> ONE PIECE PARALLEL WORLD RPG
> Folder : 01_WORLD/ISLANDS

---

# PURPOSE

ไฟล์นี้กำหนดมาตรฐานในการสร้างเกาะทั้งหมดภายใน PROJECT ASTER ENGINE

ทุกเกาะ ไม่ว่าจะเป็นเกาะจาก Canon หรือเกาะ Original ต้องใช้โครงสร้างเดียวกัน

เป้าหมายคือ

- ให้ข้อมูลเป็นมาตรฐานเดียวกัน
- ให้ AI สามารถอ่านและใช้งานได้ทันที
- ให้ผู้เล่นเดินทางไปเกาะใดก็ได้รับประสบการณ์ที่สอดคล้องกัน
- รองรับการสร้างเกาะใหม่ได้ไม่จำกัด
