# ==================================================
# SABAODY ARCHIPELAGO
# ==================================================

Sea: Paradise
Classification: Mangrove Archipelago
World Type: One Piece Parallel World

---

# OVERVIEW

Sabaody Archipelago is the final great crossroads of Paradise and the last major civilization before travelers attempt to enter the New World.

Rather than a single island, Sabaody consists of dozens of interconnected mangrove groves whose enormous roots rise above the sea, creating natural islands.

Every district is built upon the roots of giant Yarukiman Mangrove trees.

These trees continuously produce natural bubbles, allowing unique transportation, architecture, industry and technology unlike anywhere else in the world.

Because every route toward the New World passes nearby, Sabaody attracts nearly every kind of traveler imaginable.

Pirates.

Marines.

Merchants.

Treasure Hunters.

Scientists.

Slave Traders.

World Government Officials.

Foreign Nobles.

Shipwrights.

Bounty Hunters.

Information Brokers.

Criminal Syndicates.

Every day thousands of ships arrive.

Some continue toward the New World.

Many never leave.

---

# GEOGRAPHY

Island Type

Mangrove Archipelago

Number of Major Groves

79

Approximate Diameter

58 km

Terrain Distribution

45% Mangrove Roots

25% Urban Districts

15% Commercial Areas

10% Natural Forest

5% Government Facilities

---

# CLIMATE

Climate Type

Warm Tropical Maritime

Spring

Warm

Humid

Stable Weather

---

Summer

Hot

Heavy Rain

Strong Ocean Winds

---

Autumn

Pleasant

Ideal Sailing Conditions

---

Winter

Warm

Occasional Storms

Sea Fog During Morning

---

# THE YARUKIMAN MANGROVES

The entire archipelago exists because of enormous Yarukiman Mangrove trees.

Their gigantic roots emerge from the ocean floor and intertwine to create natural islands.

Every Grove is connected through these root systems.

The trees naturally generate bubbles from their resin.

These bubbles have become the foundation of local civilization.

---

## Bubble Characteristics

Naturally Produced

Extremely Light

Transparent

Elastic

Temporary

Sensitive to Sharp Objects

Can support heavy weight depending on size.

Used throughout daily life.

---

## Bubble Applications

Transportation

Housing

Architecture

Cargo

Tourism

Medical Equipment

Entertainment

Industrial Machinery

Scientific Research

---

# NATURAL RESOURCES

Primary Resources

Bubble Resin

Mangrove Wood

Fresh Water

Fish

Seafood

Medicinal Plants

Natural Rubber

Rare Flowers

---

Rare Resources

High Purity Bubble Resin

Ancient Mangrove Resin

Sea Prism Stone Fragments

Rare Marine Minerals

Deep Root Crystals

---

# NATURAL PHENOMENA

## Bubble Rain

During certain humid mornings millions of tiny bubbles drift across the archipelago.

Visibility decreases.

Children celebrate it as a seasonal event.

Scientists continue studying its cause.

---

## Root Growth

The mangrove roots slowly expand every year.

Entire districts occasionally require rebuilding.

Roads shift naturally over decades.

---

## Bubble Storm

Rare weather conditions cause unstable bubbles to explode throughout parts of the city.

Transportation temporarily shuts down.

---

## Giant Root Tremors

Massive underwater root movement occasionally causes localized earthquakes.

Buildings are specifically designed to withstand these events.

---

# GROVES 1–79 OVERVIEW SYSTEM

Sabaody Archipelago is divided into 79 Groves.

Each Grove is not just a district but a semi-independent micro-city connected through mangrove root bridges and bubble transport systems.

Below is the structural classification of all Groves.

---

# GROVE CLASSIFICATION SYSTEM

Grove 1–9  
Industrial / Ship Maintenance / Coating Supply

Grove 10–19  
Residential Housing District

Grove 20–29  
Commercial & Entertainment

Grove 30–39  
Tourism & Luxury District

Grove 40–49  
Government Administration

Grove 50–59  
Marine & Security Sector

Grove 60–69  
Science & Research

Grove 70–79  
World Government Restricted Zone

---

# END OF PART 2

# ==================================================
# SABAODY ARCHIPELAGO
# PART 3 (FINAL)
# ==================================================

# CULTURE

Sabaody Archipelago is a collision point of the entire world.

Cultures from Paradise, New World routes, and unknown islands merge into one unstable but vibrant society.

Unlike other cities, Sabaody does not have a single identity.

It is defined by arrival and departure.

People come here to leave.

Or to disappear.

---

## GENERAL CULTURE

Core Values

Freedom of Movement

Profit Above All

Information is Currency

Strength Determines Safety

Survival Through Adaptation

---

## DAILY LIFE

Morning

Ship arrivals

Coating operations begin

Market openings

Marine inspections

---

Afternoon

Trade negotiations

Tourist activity

Research operations

Transport congestion

---

Night

Black market expansion

Information trade

Illegal auctions

High-risk transactions

---

## SOCIAL STRUCTURE

World Nobles

Absolute authority within restricted zones

---

Government Officials

Administrative control class

---

Marine Officers

Military enforcement class

---

Merchants

Economic power class

---

Coating Engineers

Highly respected technical class

---

Information Brokers

Shadow influence network

---

Workers

Core labor force

---

Underground Population

Illegal traders

Escaped slaves

Criminal syndicates

---

# FOOD CULTURE

Signature Foods

Bubble Steam Seafood

Mangrove Root Soup

Coated Grilled Fish

Deep Sea Oyster Rice

Salted Bubble Fruit

---

Street Food

Fried Fish Buns

Sea Salt Skewers

Bubble Candy

Grilled Mangrove Worms (rare delicacy)

---

Luxury Dining

Imported Wines

Sea King Meat (rare)

Frozen Deep Sea Dishes

World Noble Cuisine

---

# FESTIVALS

## Arrival Festival

Celebrates ships entering Sabaody.

Entire Groves light up with floating bubbles.

---

## Bubble Storm Night

Artistic festival simulating bubble storms.

Music and light shows across Groves 20–39.

---

## Coating Ceremony

Ships preparing for Fish-Man Island receive blessing-like coating rituals.

Engineers treated like artists.

---

## Market Collapse Day

Historical remembrance of past economic crashes.

Mock auctions and trade games occur.

---

# IMPORTANT NPCs

(All original to Parallel World)

---

## Governor Elias Verdane

Role: Governor of Sabaody Archipelago

Age: 52

Personality: Calculated, diplomatic, pragmatic

Goal: Maintain stability under World Government pressure

Secret: Maintains hidden agreements with underground brokers

---

## Admiral Kairo D. Venn

Role: Marine Regional Commander

Age: 46

Personality: Strict, tactical, unpredictable

Belief: “Pirates are symptoms, not causes”

---

## Chief Engineer Mira Solenne

Role: Head of Bubble Research Institute

Age: 39

Specialty: Bubble physics and structural engineering

Goal: Create permanent bubble-based cities

---

## Broker “Silk” Ravel

Role: Information Broker Kingpin

Age: Unknown

Network: Controls most black-market intelligence flow

Rumor: Has access to Celestial Dragon schedules

---

## Auction Master Dorian Vale

Role: Illegal Auction Organizer

Age: 44

Specialty: Rare goods, human trafficking markets

Influence: Controls underground economy pricing

---

## Captain Ren Holloway

Role: Marine Intelligence Unit

Age: 35

Personality: Silent, observant

Goal: Infiltrate black market network

---

## Doctor Lysa Ardent

Role: Emergency Medical Director

Age: 41

Specialty: Trauma from piracy and ship accidents

Secret: Treats escaped slaves secretly

---

## “Root King” Unknown Entity

Role: Myth / Unknown figure

Rumor: Controls movements within mangrove root systems

No confirmed identity

Possibly non-human intelligence

---

# RUMORS

1. A hidden Grove exists beyond Grove 79, erased from all maps.

2. Certain bubbles can store human memories.

3. World Nobles travel through invisible routes inside mangrove roots.

4. A slave auction has been operating under Marine protection.

5. Some ships entering Sabaody never leave but are still “seen” at sea.

6. The mangroves may respond to human emotion.

7. A Marine admiral is secretly funding black market auctions.

8. There is a ship coating that can hide a vessel from Haki detection.

9. Information Broker “Silk” is actually multiple people sharing one identity.

10. The Root King may control all transportation bubbles.

---

# RANDOM ENCOUNTERS

Roll 1D10

| Roll | Encounter |
|------|----------|
|1|A bubble transport system collapses mid-air|
|2|Illegal auction invitation delivered to player|
|3|Marine inspection escalates into conflict|
|4|A noble arrives unexpectedly in public zone|
|5|Black market informant offers secret map|
|6|Coating engineer requests urgent escort|
|7|Slave convoy passes through hidden canal|
|8|Bubble storm traps entire district|
|9|Assassination attempt on merchant leader|
|10|Unknown figure appears inside mangrove roots|

---

# ADVENTURE HOOKS

Infiltrate illegal auctions hidden beneath Groves 20–29

Escort a World Noble through unstable Groves

Investigate disappearing ships after coating process

Expose corruption inside Marine Sabaody division

Track the identity of “Silk” the information broker

Protect engineers developing revolutionary bubble tech

Escape Sabaody while being hunted by multiple factions

Discover the truth behind Grove 79

Stop a slave trade route disguised as tourist transport

Investigate rumors of Root King intelligence

Recover stolen Marine intelligence files

Prevent assassination during Arrival Festival

---

# HIDDEN LOCATIONS

## The Forgotten Grove

A Grove erased from official records

Exists between root layers of mangroves

---

## Sub-Bubble Prison

Floating underground detention system

Used for high-value prisoners

---

## Root Network Core

Central control system of mangrove structure

Unknown origin

---

## Silent Auction Depth

Hidden auction hall beneath Groves 23–29

---

## World Noble Passage

Secret transport route inside mangrove roots

Invisible from surface navigation

---

## Coating Abyss

Deep experimental bubble pressure facility

Ships vanish during failed tests

---

# SECRETS

Sabaody is not just a transit point.

It is a filter.

The world passes through it, and only some continue forward.

Some Groves are not physically connected but still accessible.

The World Government does not fully understand the mangroves.

Some bubbles behave as if they are alive.

Certain individuals have “disappeared” but are still recorded as active travelers.

The Root King may not be a person.

It may be the system itself.

---

# GM NOTES

Recommended Campaign Role

Mid to High Paradise Transition Arc

Core Themes

Corruption

Transit Chaos

Identity Loss

Power Hierarchies

Underground Economy

Information Warfare

Survival Pressure

Mystery Systems

---

Sabaody should feel unstable.

Every encounter should carry uncertainty.

Every alliance should feel temporary.

Every escape route should feel incomplete.

This is not a city.

It is a gateway that decides who is worthy to continue the journey.

---

# END OF FILE

# ==================================================
# BATERILLA
# ==================================================

Sea: South Blue
Classification: Canon Island
World Type: One Piece Parallel World

---

# OVERVIEW

Baterilla is a peaceful island nation located within South Blue. Though modest in size and population, the island is renowned for its fertile farmland, quiet fishing villages, and unwavering community spirit.

Unlike the bustling trade cities of other Blues, Baterilla values family, craftsmanship, and agriculture above commerce. The island has historically remained politically neutral and has avoided involvement in major conflicts whenever possible.

In the Parallel World, Baterilla has become a favored destination for refugees, retired sailors, and families seeking a peaceful life away from the dangers of piracy.

---

# GEOGRAPHY

Island Size

Medium

Approximate Diameter

88 km

Travel Time by Foot

2 Days

Highest Elevation

Mount Selene

Terrain Distribution

35% Forest

25% Farmland

20% Hills

10% Rivers

10% Coastline

---

# CLIMATE

Climate Type

Temperate Maritime

Spring

Warm rain and blooming fields.

Summer

Sunny with sea breezes.

Autumn

Harvest season.

Winter

Cool with light snowfall in the mountains.

---

# NATURAL RESOURCES

Primary Resources

- Fresh Water
- Wheat
- Fruits
- Hardwood
- Fish

Rare Resources

- White Marble
- Healing Herbs

---

# MAJOR REGIONS

## Port Baterilla

Main harbor and trade center.

---

## Greenfield Plains

Largest agricultural zone.

---

## Selene Highlands

Mountain villages and forests.

---

## Eastern Orchards

Fruit plantations supplying nearby islands.

---

## Riverside Valley

Fishing villages built around freshwater rivers.

---

# SETTLEMENTS

Baterilla consists of several small communities connected by well-maintained roads.

---

## Port Baterilla

Type

Harbor Town

Population

Approximately 9,500

Description

The island's commercial heart where merchants, fishermen, and travelers gather.

Major Facilities

- Harbor
- Marketplace
- Ship Repair Yard
- Marine Office
- Hospital
- Town Hall

Primary Occupations

- Fishermen
- Merchants
- Sailors
- Shipwrights

---

## Greenfield Village

Type

Agricultural Village

Population

Approximately 5,800

Description

Known for producing grain and vegetables throughout South Blue.

Major Facilities

- Grain Mills
- Barns
- Farmers Guild
- Local School

Primary Occupations

- Farmers
- Ranchers
- Carpenters

---

## Selene Village

Type

Mountain Village

Population

Approximately 2,400

Description

Home to herbalists, hunters and miners.

Major Facilities

- Herbal Clinic
- Quarry
- Mountain Shrine

Primary Occupations

- Hunters
- Herbalists
- Stoneworkers

---

## Riverside Hamlet

Type

Fishing Community

Population

Approximately 1,600

Description

A peaceful riverside settlement famous for freshwater fish.

Major Facilities

- Fishing Dock
- Smokehouse
- Boat Workshop

Primary Occupations

- Fishermen
- Boat Builders

---

# HARBORS

## Grand Harbor

Purpose

Regional Trade

Facilities

- Commercial Pier
- Fishing Dock
- Dry Dock
- Warehouse District
- Customs Office

Average Daily Ships

35–60

---

## Riverside Fishing Port

Purpose

Freshwater & Coastal Fishing

Average Boats

90

Exports

- Fresh Fish
- Smoked Fish
- River Crab

---

# ROAD NETWORK

Road Quality

Good

Major Roads

Harvest Road

Connects Greenfield Village with Port Baterilla.

Mountain Pass

Leads to Selene Village.

River Route

Follows the island's main freshwater river.

Orchard Trail

Used by farmers transporting fruit harvests.

---

# TRANSPORTATION

Common Methods

- Walking
- Horse Cart
- River Boat
- Fishing Boat
- Merchant Ship

Travel Time

Port → Greenfield

45 Minutes

Port → Selene Village

1 Hour 30 Minutes

Greenfield → Riverside

40 Minutes

Selene → Orchard District

1 Hour

---

# POPULATION

Estimated Population

Approximately 21,500

Human

97%

Fish-Man

1%

Longarm Tribe

Rare

Longleg Tribe

Rare

Visitors

Several hundred each week.

Population Distribution

Urban

45%

Rural

55%

Average Literacy

High

Average Wealth

Moderate

Crime Rate

Low

---

# GOVERNMENT

## Political System

Baterilla is governed by an elected town council supported by village representatives from each region.

Major decisions require approval from both the council and the village assembly, creating a balance between urban and rural interests.

Departments

- Agriculture
- Harbor Administration
- Public Works
- Education
- Public Health
- Trade
- Emergency Services

---

# LAW ENFORCEMENT

## Island Guard

The Island Guard is responsible for maintaining peace across Baterilla.

Responsibilities

- Patrol Villages
- Resolve Civil Disputes
- Protect Trade Routes
- Assist During Storms
- Cooperate with Marines

Because crime is relatively uncommon, guards are well known by local residents.

---

# MARINE PRESENCE

## Baterilla Marine Branch

Classification

Branch Base

Personnel

Approximately 180 Marines

Facilities

- Headquarters
- Barracks
- Prison Cells
- Medical Clinic
- Armory
- Dock

Fleet

Patrol Boats

4

Medium Ships

2

Transport Ships

1

Responsibilities

- Anti-Piracy Patrol
- Harbor Security
- Escort Merchant Ships
- Emergency Rescue

The Marines maintain friendly relations with the civilian government and rarely interfere in local affairs unless piracy is involved.

---

# ECONOMY

## Economic Profile

Baterilla's economy revolves around agriculture and fishing rather than heavy industry.

Economic Stability

★★★★☆

Currency

Belly

Employment

High

Primary Economic Drivers

- Agriculture
- Fruit Farming
- Fishing
- Local Trade
- Livestock
- Timber

---

# MAJOR INDUSTRIES

## Agriculture

The island's fertile plains produce large harvests every season.

Major Crops

- Wheat
- Rice
- Corn
- Potatoes
- Vegetables

---

## Fruit Production

The Eastern Orchards produce premium fruits exported across South Blue.

Popular Products

- Apples
- Citrus
- Grapes
- Pears

---

## Fishing

Fishing families have operated for generations.

Common Catch

- Mackerel
- Snapper
- River Trout
- Crab
- Shrimp

---

## Forestry

Managed forests provide sustainable timber for construction and ship repair.

Products

- Hardwood
- Firewood
- Lumber

---

# TRADE

## Major Exports

Agricultural Goods

- Wheat
- Rice
- Fruit
- Honey

Seafood

- Fresh Fish
- Smoked Fish
- Salted Seafood

Craft Goods

- Furniture
- Wooden Barrels
- Farming Tools

---

## Major Imports

Industrial Goods

- Steel
- Machinery
- Glass

Luxury Goods

- Wine
- Silk
- Jewelry

Medical Goods

- Surgical Equipment
- Imported Medicine
- Chemicals

---

# CULTURE

## General Culture

Baterilla values family, honesty, and mutual support above personal wealth.

Most residents know each other by name, and strangers are usually welcomed warmly unless they behave suspiciously.

People believe that a peaceful community is stronger than a wealthy one.

---

## Hospitality

Visitors are often invited to share meals with local families.

Travelers staying for several days frequently receive assistance without asking, especially during harvest season.

---

## Social Classes

### Farmers

The backbone of the island's economy.

Highly respected for providing food to the entire population.

---

### Fishermen

Operate both river and coastal fisheries.

Families often pass down boats through generations.

---

### Craftsmen

Known for woodworking, carpentry, and furniture making.

Many shipwrights begin their careers here before moving to larger ports.

---

### Merchants

Responsible for exporting agricultural products throughout South Blue.

---

# EDUCATION

Public education is available in every major settlement.

Core Subjects

- Reading
- Writing
- Mathematics
- Geography
- Agriculture
- Navigation
- History

Many children help their families after school in farms or fishing boats.

---

# RELIGION

No official religion exists.

Residents commonly visit small shrines dedicated to

- Ancestors
- The Sea
- Harvest Spirits

Annual ceremonies are held before planting and harvesting seasons.

---

# FESTIVALS

## Spring Planting Festival

Celebrates the beginning of the farming season.

Activities

- Music
- Dancing
- Community Feast
- Seed Blessing

---

## Harvest Festival

Held after the autumn harvest.

Activities

- Cooking Competitions
- Fruit Market
- Traditional Games
- Lantern Parade

---

## Fisherman's Tide Festival

Celebrates the beginning of the summer fishing season.

Activities

- Boat Parade
- Net Throwing Competition
- Seafood Fair

---

# FOOD

Common Meals

Breakfast

- Bread
- Fresh Milk
- Eggs

Lunch

- Rice
- Vegetables
- Grilled Fish

Dinner

- Beef Stew
- Roasted Chicken
- Seasonal Fruits

---

## Local Specialties

Baterilla Farm Stew

A hearty stew made from vegetables, beef and herbs.

---

Honey Wheat Bread

Freshly baked bread served with locally produced honey.

---

River Trout Grill

Grilled freshwater trout seasoned with mountain herbs.

---

Orchard Fruit Pie

Fruit pie made from apples and pears harvested on the island.

---

# FLORA

Common Trees

- Oak
- Maple
- Cedar
- Apple Tree
- Pear Tree

Medicinal Plants

- Silver Leaf
- Green Mint
- Sun Herb

Rare Plants

- Moon Blossom
- Golden Fern

---

# WILDLIFE

Harmless Animals

- Deer
- Rabbits
- Foxes
- Wild Goats

Farm Animals

- Cattle
- Chickens
- Sheep
- Horses

Marine Life

- Trout
- Snapper
- Crab
- Shrimp

Dangerous Animals

---

# NATURAL HAZARDS

Possible environmental dangers

- Heavy Rain
- Seasonal Floods
- Mountain Landslides
- Strong Coastal Winds

The island rarely experiences catastrophic disasters.

---

# SECURITY LEVEL

Port Baterilla

★★★★☆

Greenfield Village

★★★★★

Selene Village

★★★★☆

Eastern Orchards

★★★★☆

Riverside Hamlet

★★★★★

Mountain Forest

★★★☆☆

Remote Hills

★★★☆☆

---

# CRIME

Overall Crime Level

Low

Most crimes involve petty theft or trade disputes rather than violence.

Common Crimes

- Pickpocketing
- Illegal Hunting
- Tax Evasion
- Smuggling Small Goods

Organized crime has little influence due to the close-knit population.

---

# BLACK MARKET

Very Small

Illegal trade occasionally appears near abandoned coastal caves.

Common Contraband

- Stolen Livestock
- Untaxed Alcohol
- Smuggled Medicine

Most operations are quickly shut down by local authorities.

---

# PIRATE ACTIVITY

Pirate Threat Level

Low

Most pirate crews bypass Baterilla because the island offers few valuable targets.

Occasionally small crews stop only for food and fresh water.

Typical Crew Size

5–20 Pirates

Average Bounty

2,000,000–8,000,000 Belly

---

# IMPORTANT ORGANIZATIONS

## Farmers Guild

Influence

★★★★★

Coordinates crop production, irrigation projects, and grain distribution across the island.

---

## Fishermen Association

Influence

★★★★☆

Maintains fishing regulations, protects spawning grounds, and organizes rescue operations during storms.

---

## Carpenter Cooperative

Influence

★★★★☆

Produces furniture, farming equipment, wagons, and small fishing vessels.

---

## Medical Society

Influence

★★★☆☆

Operates village clinics and trains herbalists throughout Baterilla.

---

# IMPORTANT NPCS

## Mayor Elias Rowan

Occupation

Mayor of Port Baterilla

Age

58

Personality

Patient

Practical

Kind

Description

A respected administrator who believes prosperity begins with stable families and honest work.

---

## Captain Victor Hale

Occupation

Marine Branch Commander

Age

42

Justice

Protective Justice

Description

Places civilian safety above personal recognition and regularly joins rescue missions during storms.

---

## Doctor Amelia Voss

Occupation

Chief Physician

Age

39

Specialty

Herbal Medicine

Emergency Care

Description

Combines modern medicine with traditional herbal remedies gathered from Mount Selene.

---

## Rowan Mercer

Occupation

Guildmaster of the Farmers Guild

Age

61

Description

A veteran farmer whose advice influences nearly every agricultural decision on the island.

---

## Isaac Moore

Occupation

Master Shipwright

Age

54

Description

Builds durable fishing boats renowned throughout South Blue.

---

# RUMORS

1. Strange lights appear deep inside Mount Selene.
2. An abandoned farmhouse hides forgotten tunnels.
3. Rare herbs capable of curing deadly diseases bloom only once every decade.
4. A pirate buried stolen treasure somewhere along the northern cliffs.
5. Livestock occasionally disappears without leaving tracks.
6. Old ruins have recently been uncovered beneath farmland.
7. A mysterious traveler purchases ancient family heirlooms for enormous sums.
8. Fishermen report hearing bells beneath the sea during calm nights.
9. A hidden spring is said to heal any illness.
10. Someone has been secretly buying large quantities of grain.

---

# RANDOM ENCOUNTERS

Roll 1D10

| Roll | Encounter |
|------|-----------|
|1|A farmer requests help repairing a windmill.|
|2|Children chase escaped sheep across the road.|
|3|A traveling merchant offers discounted supplies.|
|4|Marines inspect passing cargo wagons.|
|5|A fisherman asks for assistance hauling in an enormous catch.|
|6|Wild boars damage nearby farmland.|
|7|Heavy rain floods the road ahead.|
|8|An herbalist searches for a rare medicinal flower.|
|9|Villagers prepare for a local festival.|
|10|A suspicious stranger quietly observes the party.|

---

# ADVENTURE HOOKS

- Investigate disappearing livestock around Greenfield.
- Escort this year's harvest shipment safely across South Blue.
- Discover who is purchasing enormous food reserves.
- Explore newly uncovered ruins beneath the farmland.
- Recover stolen medicine before an epidemic spreads.
- Rescue missing herbalists from Mount Selene.
- Stop smugglers using abandoned sea caves.
- Search for the legendary Healing Spring.

---

# HIDDEN LOCATIONS

## Forgotten Chapel

An abandoned stone chapel hidden inside the forest.

---

## Selene Caverns

Natural caves beneath Mount Selene rich in medicinal plants.

---

## Ancient Irrigation Tunnel

A centuries-old underground water system still partially functional.

---

## Hidden Smuggler Cove

A concealed beach accessible only during low tide.

---

# SECRETS

Several ancient ruins beneath Greenfield predate the current kingdom by hundreds of years.

The Healing Spring truly exists, but its water loses its properties shortly after leaving the source.

One influential merchant secretly finances smugglers to avoid export taxes.

The caves beneath Mount Selene connect to forgotten tunnels extending beneath half the island.

---

# GM NOTES

Recommended Campaign Length

2–4 Sessions

Recommended Player Level

Beginning Characters

Core Themes

- Peaceful Exploration
- Community
- Agriculture
- Family
- Mystery

## GM Guidance

Baterilla should feel like a place worth protecting rather than conquering.

Conflicts here are personal and community-driven instead of world-shaking, making it an excellent location for introducing roleplay, investigation, and emotional storytelling before players enter more dangerous seas.

# END OF FILE

# =====================================================
# FILE : 01_WORLD\ISLANDS\SOUTH_BLUE\SB-002_TORINO_KINGDOM.md
# =====================================================

# ==================================================
# TORINO KINGDOM
# ==================================================

Sea: South Blue
Classification: Canon Island
World Type: One Piece Parallel World

---

# OVERVIEW

Torino Kingdom is a remote island located deep within South Blue, famous for its enormous forests, towering trees, unusual wildlife, and the harmonious relationship between its inhabitants and nature.

Unlike most kingdoms, Torino has never pursued military expansion or commercial dominance. Its greatest treasures are knowledge of medicine, rare plants, and ecological preservation.

In the Parallel World, Torino has become one of the world's leading centers for botanical research. Doctors, herbalists, scholars, and explorers travel here to study plants found nowhere else in the world.

---

# GEOGRAPHY

Island Size

Medium

Approximate Diameter

95 km

Travel Time by Foot

2–3 Days

Highest Elevation

Great Canopy Mountain

Terrain Distribution

55% Forest

20% Mountains

10% Grassland

10% Rivers

5% Settlements

---

# CLIMATE

Climate Type

Humid Tropical

Spring

Warm and rainy.

Summer

Hot with frequent thunderstorms.

Autumn

Comfortable temperatures and abundant harvests.

Winter

Cool mornings with dense fog.

---

# NATURAL RESOURCES

Primary Resources

- Medicinal Herbs
- Hardwood
- Fresh Water
- Wild Fruits
- Mushrooms

Rare Resources

- Eternal Bloom Flower
- Scarlet Moss
- Golden Sap
- Moon Orchid
- Ancient Medicinal Vines

---

# MAJOR REGIONS

## Torino Village

Capital settlement built among giant trees.

---

## Great Canopy Forest

The island's largest rainforest.

---

## Botanical Sanctuary

Protected research zone containing hundreds of rare plants.

---

## Mist River

Freshwater river crossing the island.

---

## Emerald Highlands

Mountain region containing caves and waterfalls.

---

# SETTLEMENTS

## Torino Village

Type

Capital Village

Population

Approximately 6,500

Description

Wooden homes connected by bridges and elevated walkways among gigantic trees.

Major Facilities

- Royal Hall
- Medical Academy
- Botanical Library
- Marketplace
- Herbal Hospital

Primary Occupations

- Herbalists
- Doctors
- Researchers
- Hunters

---

## Greenroot Hamlet

Population

1,900

Description

Agricultural settlement producing medicinal crops and fruits.

---

## Highlands Camp

Population

800

Description

Research outpost dedicated to studying rare mountain plants.

# POPULATION

Estimated Population

Approximately 9,500

Human

88%

Longarm Tribe

5%

Dwarves

Rare

Visitors

Very Few

Very High

Crime Rate

Very Low

# GOVERNMENT

Political System

Constitutional Kingdom

King, Royal Council, Botanical Council

- Medicine
- Environmental Protection
- Education
- Public Safety

★★★★★

---

The people of Torino believe every living organism has a role within nature.

Children begin learning plant identification from an early age.

Cutting healthy trees without permission is considered a serious offense.

Knowledge is regarded as the kingdom's greatest wealth.

---

# EDUCATION

Education focuses on

- Biology
- Medicine
- Botany
- Geography
- Survival
- Ecology

Graduates from the Medical Academy are respected throughout South Blue.

★★★★★

★★★★★

Forest

★★★☆☆

Influence

★★★★★

---

Produces physicians and researchers serving every Blue.

Influence

★★★★★

---

Controls cultivation and export of medicinal plants.

Influence

★★★★☆

---

# IMPORTANT NPCS

## King Aldren Torino

Occupation

King

Age

62

Description

A wise monarch devoted to preserving nature.

---

Occupation

Director of Medical Academy

Age

58

Description

One of South Blue's leading botanists.

Royal Ranger Commander

44

Occupation

Chief Physician

Age

39

Description

---

# RUMORS

1. A flower capable of extending life blooms once every hundred years.
2. Strange lights appear within the Great Canopy Forest.
3. Ancient ruins lie beneath the Botanical Sanctuary.
4. Poachers have begun targeting protected wildlife.
5. A forgotten laboratory exists inside the mountains.
6. Some herbs only bloom under a full moon.
7. A gigantic creature guards the deepest forest.
8. Someone has been stealing research documents.
9. Rare seeds are being smuggled overseas.
10. An unknown disease has begun affecting ancient trees.

---

# RANDOM ENCOUNTERS

| Roll | Encounter |
|------|-----------|
|1|Researchers request assistance collecting herbs.|
|2|A wounded animal blocks the trail.|
|3|Royal Rangers inspect travelers.|
|4|A rare flower blooms nearby.|
|5|Heavy rain floods the forest path.|
|6|A poisonous snake attacks.|
|7|Children gather medicinal plants.|
|8|A merchant seeks botanical samples.|
|9|An abandoned research camp is discovered.|
|10|A mysterious herbalist offers unusual medicine.|

---

# ADVENTURE HOOKS

- Stop an illegal plant-smuggling operation.
- Search for the legendary Eternal Bloom Flower.
- Escort researchers into unexplored forests.
- Investigate mysterious plant mutations.
- Rescue missing botanists.
- Explore forgotten ruins beneath the sanctuary.
- Capture wildlife poachers.
- Cure a disease spreading among ancient trees.

---

# HIDDEN LOCATIONS

## Ancient Greenhouse

A forgotten botanical laboratory buried beneath the forest.

---

## Moon Orchid Valley

Accessible only during specific lunar cycles.

---

## Emerald Caverns

Underground caves containing glowing medicinal crystals.

---

## Forgotten Temple

Ancient shrine dedicated to nature spirits.

---

# SECRETS

Several medicinal plants found in Torino cannot be cultivated anywhere else in the world.

Ancient ruins beneath the Botanical Sanctuary predate the current kingdom by centuries.

The Royal Medical Academy secretly preserves extinct plant species collected from every sea.

---

# GM NOTES

Recommended Campaign Length

2–3 Sessions

Recommended Player Level

Beginning–Intermediate

Core Themes

- Exploration
- Nature
- Medicine
- Survival
- Scientific Discovery

## GM Guidance

Torino Kingdom is ideal for slower-paced adventures focused on discovery, healing, environmental mysteries, and roleplaying. Combat should be secondary to exploration, investigation, and interaction with the island's unique ecosystem.

# END OF FILE

# FILE : 01_WORLD\ISLANDS\SOUTH_BLUE\SB-003_SUNBURST_ISLAND.md
# =====================================================

# ==================================================
# SUNBURST ISLAND
# ==================================================

Sea: South Blue
Classification: Original Island
World Type: One Piece Parallel World

---

# OVERVIEW

Sunburst Island is a prosperous tropical island famous for its nearly year-round sunshine, fertile volcanic soil, and vast citrus plantations. Nicknamed "The Island of Endless Summer," it supplies fruit, sugar, and tropical products throughout South Blue.

Although peaceful on the surface, competition between merchant companies and fruit exporters creates constant political tension beneath everyday life.

---

# GEOGRAPHY

Island Size

Medium

Approximate Diameter

110 km

Travel Time by Foot

2–3 Days

Highest Elevation

Mount Solis

Terrain Distribution

40% Plantation

25% Forest

15% Hills

10% Coastline

10% Towns

---

# CLIMATE

Climate Type

Tropical

Spring

Warm and humid.

Summer

Very hot with frequent sunshine.

Autumn

Short rainy season.

Winter

Mild temperatures.

---

# NATURAL RESOURCES

Primary Resources

- Citrus Fruits
- Sugar Cane
- Hardwood
- Fresh Water
- Fish

Rare Resources

- Golden Citrus
- Sunstone Crystal

---

# MAJOR REGIONS

## Sunport City

Capital and commercial center.

---

## Golden Orchard

Largest fruit plantation.

---

## Mount Solis

Dormant volcano covered in forests.

---

## South Coast

Fishing communities and beaches.

---

## Crystal River

Main freshwater source.

---

- Hospital
- Guild Hall

43

Chief Physician

Description

# RUMORS

1. A legendary Golden Citrus tree still exists.
2. Smugglers use abandoned irrigation tunnels.
3. Pirates seek a hidden warehouse full of rare fruit.
4. Strange tremors have begun beneath Mount Solis.
5. A merchant family controls prices illegally.
6. Ancient ruins lie inside the volcano.
7. A mysterious botanist disappeared decades ago.
8. Rare birds have stopped nesting this year.
9. Someone is poisoning citrus orchards.
10. A forgotten treasure map is hidden inside an old winery.

---

# RANDOM ENCOUNTERS

| Roll | Encounter |
|------|-----------|
|1|Workers harvesting oranges.|
|2|Merchant caravan requests escorts.|
|3|Marines inspect cargo.|
|4|Wild monkeys steal supplies.|
|5|A sudden tropical storm arrives.|
|6|Children compete in a fruit festival.|
|7|A rare Golden Citrus tree is discovered.|
|8|Smugglers flee into nearby orchards.|
|9|A doctor searches for medicinal fruit.|
|10|A volcanic tremor shakes the island.|

---

# ADVENTURE HOOKS

- Protect this year's fruit harvest.
- Stop smugglers operating beneath the plantations.
- Investigate volcanic activity.
- Recover a stolen trade contract.
- Search for the legendary Golden Citrus Tree.
- Uncover corruption within the Fruit Export Guild.
- Explore forgotten ruins beneath Mount Solis.
- Capture pirates targeting merchant convoys.

---

# HIDDEN LOCATIONS

## Forgotten Winery

An abandoned underground storage cellar.

---

## Volcanic Caverns

Natural lava tunnels beneath Mount Solis.

---

## Hidden Orchard

An untouched grove producing mythical Golden Citrus fruit.

---

## Smuggler Tunnel

Ancient irrigation tunnels converted into secret trade routes.

---

# SECRETS

The Fruit Export Guild secretly manipulates market prices across South Blue.

Golden Citrus trees naturally grow only in volcanic soil beneath Mount Solis.

Ancient ruins beneath the volcano belong to a civilization predating the current kingdom.

---

# GM NOTES

Recommended Campaign Length

2–3 Sessions

Recommended Player Level

Beginning–Intermediate

Core Themes

- Trade
- Agriculture
- Exploration
- Mystery
- Economic Conflict

## GM Guidance

Sunburst Island provides a balance between peaceful roleplay and commercial intrigue. Most adventures revolve around protecting trade, uncovering corruption, and exploring the dormant volcano that quietly dominates the island.

# END OF FILE

# FILE : 01_WORLD\ISLANDS\SOUTH_BLUE\SB-004_CORALHAVEN.md
# =====================================================

# ==================================================
# CORALHAVEN
# ==================================================

Sea: South Blue
Classification: Original Island
World Type: One Piece Parallel World

---

# OVERVIEW

Coralhaven is an island built around massive coral reef systems that extend both above and below sea level. The island is famous for its marine biodiversity, underwater settlements, and advanced diving culture.

It is one of the few islands in South Blue where humans, Fish-Men, and other sea-adapted species coexist closely due to the island’s unique semi-aquatic environment.

---

# GEOGRAPHY

Island Size

Medium

Approximate Diameter

85 km

Travel Time by Foot

2 Days (land only)

Highest Elevation

Coral Spire Ridge

Terrain Distribution

30% Coral Reef Structures

25% Coastal Towns

20% Shallow Waters

15% Islands

10% Underwater Caverns

---

# CLIMATE

Climate Type

Warm Oceanic

Year-round Conditions

Stable warm waters with mild surface winds.

Storm Season

Strong underwater currents and surface storms.

---

# NATURAL RESOURCES

Primary Resources

- Coral Stone
- Pearls
- Marine Minerals
- Fish
- Sea Plants

Rare Resources

- Blue Coral Crystal
- Deep Pearl
- Luminescent Algae

---

# MAJOR REGIONS

## Coralport City

Main hub and trading port.

---

## Reef District

Half-submerged residential area.

---

## Deepwater Trench

Underwater canyon system.

---

## Pearl Fields

Natural pearl farming zones.

---

## Drift Isles

Floating small islands around the main landmass.

---

# SETTLEMENTS

## Coralport City

Population

Approximately 10,500

Description

A hybrid land-sea city with docks, underwater tunnels, and coral-built architecture.

Facilities

- Harbor
- Marine Station
- Pearl Market
- Diving Guild Hall
- Hospital
- Trade Center

---

## Reef District

Population

6,200

Description

Residential zone partially submerged underwater.

Homes are built inside reinforced coral structures and glass domes.

Population

2,100

Small fishing communities on floating islands.

Influence

★★★★☆

Environmental enforcement organization.

Age

46

Role

# RUMORS

1. A sunken ship contains an ancient weapon.
2. Deep trenches hide unknown civilizations.
3. Pearl farms are being sabotaged.
4. A giant creature guards underwater ruins.
5. Illegal submarine routes are expanding.
6. A lost diver keeps returning alive without oxygen gear.
7. Strange glowing coral is spreading rapidly.
8. A hidden underwater city may exist.
9. Fish-Man rebels are recruiting in secret.
10. A Marine submarine disappeared near the trench.

---

# RANDOM ENCOUNTERS

| Roll | Encounter |
|------|-----------|
|1|Diving expedition requests help.|
|2|Pearl traders offer rare goods.|
|3|Underwater tremor shakes reef.|
|4|Marine inspection patrol.|
|5|Lost diver rescued near reef.|
|6|Sea creature attacks fishing zone.|
|7|Smugglers escape underwater tunnel.|
|8|Coral bleaching event detected.|
|9|Ancient ruins discovered underwater.|
|10|Mysterious sonar signals detected.|

---

# ADVENTURE HOOKS

- Investigate missing divers.
- Stop illegal pearl smuggling networks.
- Explore deep trench ruins.
- Protect coral ecosystems from sabotage.
- Track a rogue submarine route.
- Discover lost underwater civilization.
- Escort research teams into deep sea zones.

---

# HIDDEN LOCATIONS

## Sunken Temple

Ancient ruins beneath Deepwater Trench.

---

## Smuggler Sub-Tunnels

Hidden underwater passageways used for illegal trade.

---

## Crystal Reef Core

Rare glowing coral formation deep underground.

---

## Lost Submarine Graveyard

Dozens of wrecked vessels resting on the seabed.

---

# SECRETS

Coralhaven sits above an ancient submerged city from a forgotten era.

Some coral formations are actually biological structures capable of growth and movement.

A hidden Marine experiment base exists within the Deepwater Trench.

---

# GM NOTES

Recommended Campaign Length

2–4 Sessions

Recommended Player Level

Intermediate

Core Themes

- Exploration
- Marine Mystery
- Trade Conflict
- Underwater Survival

## GM Guidance

Coralhaven is ideal for underwater exploration arcs, smuggling investigations, and introducing deep-sea mythology. The environment itself is a major threat as much as enemies.

# END OF FILE

# =====================================================
# FILE : 01_WORLD\ISLANDS\SOUTH_BLUE\SB-005_RED_LAGOON.md
# =====================================================

# ==================================================
# RED LAGOON
# ==================================================

Sea: South Blue
Classification: Original Island
World Type: One Piece Parallel World

---

# OVERVIEW

Red Lagoon is a lawless island formed around a massive blood-red saltwater lagoon surrounded by volcanic cliffs. It is infamous as a haven for pirates, smugglers, bounty hunters, and underground brokers.

Unlike most islands, Red Lagoon has no central government. Instead, it is controlled indirectly by competing criminal syndicates that maintain a fragile balance of power.

The island is often referred to as **"South Blue’s Shadow Market"**.

---

# GEOGRAPHY

Island Size

Medium

Approximate Diameter

92 km

Travel Time by Foot

2–3 Days

Highest Elevation

Crimson Cliffs

Terrain Distribution

30% Volcanic Rock

25% Urban Slums

20% Coastal Caves

15% Lagoon Waters

10% Hidden Facilities

---

# CLIMATE

Climate Type

Volcanic Coastal

Conditions

- Warm air temperature
- High humidity
- Frequent ash winds from dormant vents
- Unstable weather patterns

---

# NATURAL RESOURCES

Primary Resources

- Salt Crystals
- Volcanic Glass
- Rare Minerals
- Fish (lagoon species)

Rare Resources

- Blood Coral
- Crimson Ore
- Black Salt

---

# MAJOR REGIONS

## Black Market Central

Main illegal trade hub.

---

## Crimson Harbor

Main docking area for pirate ships.

---

## Ash District

Slum region built on volcanic rock.

---

## Hidden Lagoon Network

Submerged cave system used for smuggling.

---

## Smuggler’s Ridge

High ground used for surveillance and auctions.

---

Population

A chaotic trading zone where anything can be bought or sold.

Specialist in treating illegal combat injuries.

---

# RUMORS

1. A Devil Fruit is being auctioned in secret.
2. A hidden Marine base exists under Crimson Harbor.
3. One syndicate plans to unify the island.
4. A submerged vault contains lost treasure fleets.
5. A “ghost ship” appears in the lagoon at night.
6. A map to the Grand Line’s illegal routes is circulating.
7. Someone is creating artificial Devil Fruit replicas.
8. A syndicate leader is actually a former noble.
9. An ancient weapon is buried beneath volcanic rock.
10. The lagoon water turns black during certain moons.

---

# RANDOM ENCOUNTERS

| Roll | Encounter |
|------|-----------|
|1|Syndicate patrol demands payment.|
|2|Illegal auction underway nearby.|
|3|Assassination attempt in public street.|
|4|Marine spy observed nearby.|
|5|Smuggler offers dangerous job.|
|6|Fight breaks out between factions.|
|7|Crimson storm floods lower districts.|
|8|A rare weapon is being sold.|
|9|Hidden tunnel collapse.|
|10|Unknown Devil Fruit user appears.|

---

# ADVENTURE HOOKS

- Infiltrate a syndicate auction.
- Track illegal Devil Fruit trade.
- Rescue hostage from Crimson Fang.
- Expose undercover Marine operation.
- Map hidden lagoon tunnels.
- Prevent syndicate war escalation.
- Recover stolen World Government cargo.

---

# HIDDEN LOCATIONS

## Sunken Vault

Ancient submerged vault beneath lagoon.

---

## Crimson Cathedral

Abandoned structure used for secret meetings.

---

## Smuggler Deep Tunnels

Network of underwater passageways.

---

## Black Market Core

Central hub of illegal operations.

---

# SECRETS

Red Lagoon sits above remnants of a destroyed ancient port city.

Multiple syndicates secretly answer to an unknown higher authority.

A Devil Fruit research operation is being conducted underground.

---

# GM NOTES

Recommended Campaign Length

3–5 Sessions

Recommended Player Level

Intermediate–Advanced

Core Themes

- Crime
- Survival
- Espionage
- Moral Conflict
- Power Struggle

## GM Guidance

Red Lagoon is a high-risk sandbox. Players should feel constant tension, shifting alliances, and unpredictable danger. Nothing is stable here, and every action has political consequences.

# END OF FILE

# =====================================================
# FILE : 01_WORLD\ISLANDS\SOUTH_BLUE\SB-006_AURORA_PORT.md
# =====================================================

# ==================================================
# AURORA PORT
# ==================================================

Sea: South Blue
Classification: Original Island
World Type: One Piece Parallel World

---

# OVERVIEW

Aurora Port is a northern maritime hub famous for its rare phenomenon: glowing sea fog and bioluminescent waters that illuminate the entire coastline at night.

It is one of South Blue’s most important cold-water trade ports, connecting southern routes with northern shipping lanes and acting as a key transfer point for navigation toward the Grand Line approach routes.

The island is known for its disciplined naval presence, advanced navigation science, and strict trade regulation.

---

# GEOGRAPHY

Island Size

Medium–Large

Approximate Diameter

105 km

Travel Time by Foot

3 Days

Highest Elevation

Glacier Crown Ridge

Terrain Distribution

30% Frozen Highlands

25% Coastal Port Cities

20% Boreal Forest

15% Ice Rivers

10% Cliffs

---

# CLIMATE

Climate Type

Cold Maritime

Spring

Meltwater floods and fog.

Summer

Cool and stable.

Autumn

Strong winds and storms.

Winter

Harsh snowstorms and frozen seas.

---

# NATURAL RESOURCES

Primary Resources

- Ice Crystals
- Coldwater Fish
- Timber
- Navigation Minerals
- Salt

Rare Resources

- Aurora Ore
- Glow Ice
- Frozen Coral

---

# MAJOR REGIONS

## Aurora Harbor City

Main trade and naval hub.

---

## Frozen Trade District

Commercial center specializing in cold-region goods.

---

## Glow Bay

Coastal area with bioluminescent waters.

---

## Northern Icefields

Expansive frozen wilderness.

---

## Navigation Research Zone

Scientific facility for ocean mapping.

---

# SETTLEMENTS

## Aurora Harbor City

Population

Approximately 14,500

Description

A fortified harbor city with heat-resistant infrastructure designed to operate in freezing conditions.

Facilities

- Grand Harbor
- Naval Base
- Trade Exchange
- Navigation Institute
- Medical Center

---

## Frostwatch Village

Population

3,800

Description

Small fishing and hunting settlement near ice rivers.

---

## Northwind Outpost

Population

2,100

Description

Military-scientific station used for navigation research.

Low

- Port Security
- Emergency Response

The glowing sea phenomenon is considered a spiritual symbol of safe passage.

3. An uncharted ice route leads directly to the Grand Line.
4. A Marine ship vanished during aurora storms.
5. Illegal navigation charts are being sold secretly.
6. Something moves beneath the frozen sea.
7. A lost explorer still sends signals from icefields.
8. Aurora lights react to Devil Fruit abilities.
9. A hidden bunker exists under Northwind Outpost.
10. A forbidden sea route opens once every decade.

---

# RANDOM ENCOUNTERS

| Roll | Encounter |
|------|-----------|
|1|Ice storm forces shelter.|
|2|Navigation team requests escort.|
|3|Marine inspection checkpoint.|
|4|Frozen wreck discovered.|
|5|Aurora light anomaly appears.|
|6|Smugglers fleeing ice route.|
|7|Rescue mission for stranded sailors.|
|8|Ice serpent attack.|
|9|Lost cartography documents found.|
|10|Mysterious glowing sea path appears.|

---

# ADVENTURE HOOKS

- Investigate missing navigation ships.
- Escort explorers into icefields.
- Stop illegal chart trading networks.
- Discover hidden ice route to Grand Line.
- Rescue trapped expedition crew.
- Study Aurora anomalies affecting navigation.
- Expose corruption in Ice Trade Consortium.

---

# HIDDEN LOCATIONS

## Frozen Ancient Ship

A massive ship embedded in glacier ice.

---

## Glow Bay Map Formation

Natural bioluminescent patterns forming map-like shapes.

---

## Northwind Sub-Bunker

Hidden facility beneath research outpost.

---

## Ice Trench Route

Dangerous but potentially valuable navigation path.

---

# SECRETS

Aurora Port’s glow phenomenon is linked to unknown mineral activity deep beneath the seabed.

Some ice formations contain preserved ancient logs from lost civilizations.

A classified Marine project studies Devil Fruit interaction with aurora fields.

---

# GM NOTES

Recommended Campaign Length

2–4 Sessions

Recommended Player Level

Intermediate

Core Themes

- Navigation
- Survival
- Mystery
- Exploration
- Maritime Strategy

## GM Guidance

Aurora Port is a high-value strategic hub. Use it to introduce navigation lore, lost routes, and large-scale maritime politics. Environmental hazards should shape most encounters.

# END OF FILE

# FILE : 01_WORLD\ISLANDS\SOUTH_BLUE\SB-007_BLOOD_MANGROVE.md
# =====================================================

# ==================================================
# BLOOD MANGROVE
# ==================================================

Sea: South Blue
Classification: Forbidden Island
World Type: One Piece Parallel World

---

# OVERVIEW

Blood Mangrove is a forbidden swamp island covered entirely by massive crimson mangrove forests that bleed red resin when cut.

The island is feared across South Blue due to its toxic environment, extreme disease outbreaks, and the presence of mutated wildlife.

It is officially marked as **“No-Entry Zone”** by multiple Marine divisions.

Rumors claim the island was once used for illegal biological experiments during early World Government expansion.

---

# GEOGRAPHY

Island Size

Medium

Approximate Diameter

100 km

Travel Time by Foot

Extremely Difficult (3–5 Days)

Highest Elevation

Rotwood Spine Ridge

Terrain Distribution

45% Mangrove Swamp

20% Poison Marsh

15% Dense Jungle

10% Abandoned Ruins

10% Red Water Channels

---

# CLIMATE

Climate Type

Toxic Tropical Swamp

Conditions

- High humidity
- Toxic spores in air
- Blood-red mist at night
- Frequent swamp storms

---

# NATURAL RESOURCES

Primary Resources

- Toxic Resin
- Medicinal Contradiction Herbs (dangerous but valuable)
- Swamp Gas Minerals
- Rare Biological Samples

Rare Resources

- Crimson Sap Core
- Mutated Lotus Flower
- Blood Mangrove Heartwood

---

# MAJOR REGIONS

## Crimson Root Basin

Central swamp network.

---

## Rotwood Ruins

Ancient destroyed settlement.

---

## Poisonmist Marsh

Highly toxic fog zone.

---

## Hollow Mangrove Forest

Dense forest with unnatural growth patterns.

---

## Redwater Channels

Slow-moving blood-colored rivers.

Population

Unknown (abandoned)

★★★★☆

★★☆☆☆

★☆☆☆☆ (extreme danger)

Undefined (lawless environment)

- Mercenary extraction missions

Influence

Unknown

Believed to be remnants of a failed government experiment project.

Influence

★★★★☆

Influence

★★★☆☆

Role

Ex-research lead (wanted)

Age

Unknown

Description

## Captain Harlan Vein

Role

Marine Quarantine Commander

Age

45

Description

Enforces strict containment protocols.

---

## “Rotmother” Entity

Role

Unknown biological organism

Description

A rumored sentient mass of mangrove roots controlling parts of the swamp.

---

# RUMORS

1. The island is alive and reacts to intruders.
2. A Marine experiment created the Blood Mangroves.
3. A Devil Fruit is growing inside the swamp itself.
4. Entire expedition teams have been absorbed by the forest.
5. A hidden lab still functions underground.
6. The Rotmother controls mutated creatures.
7. Blood Mangrove sap can enhance Devil Fruit effects.
8. A lost World Government weapon is buried here.
9. Survivors hear voices from the trees.
10. The swamp expands every year.

---

# RANDOM ENCOUNTERS

| Roll | Encounter |
|------|-----------|
|1|Toxic fog reduces visibility.|
|2|Mutated crocodile attack.|
|3|Lost expedition survivor.|
|4|Abandoned lab discovered.|
|5|Swamp gas explosion.|
|6|Mercenary ambush.|
|7|Strange tree movement observed.|
|8|Infected wildlife herd.|
|9|Quarantine Marine patrol spotted.|
|10|Unknown organism contact event.|

---

# ADVENTURE HOOKS

- Investigate lost Marine expedition.
- Retrieve samples from Rotwood Ruins.
- Destroy illegal research facility.
- Survive deep swamp traversal.
- Locate Dr. Marrow Vex.
- Study Blood Mangrove mutation effects.
- Escape quarantine containment breach.

---

# HIDDEN LOCATIONS

## Submerged Research Lab

Hidden beneath swamp waters.

---

## Rotmother Core Chamber

Central mass of unknown biological entity.

---

## Experimental Vault

Contains sealed biological weapons.

---

## Mangrove Heart

Believed source of island’s mutation.

---

# SECRETS

Blood Mangrove may be a living ecosystem engineered rather than naturally evolved.

Several Marine governments deny any involvement despite evidence of experiments.

Some mutations show signs of intelligence development beyond animal level.

---

# GM NOTES

Recommended Campaign Length

3–5 Sessions

Recommended Player Level

Advanced

Core Themes

- Horror
- Survival
- Biological Mystery
- Illegal Science
- Isolation

## GM Guidance

This island is NOT meant for standard exploration. Treat it as a high-risk narrative arc with paranoia, environmental danger, and unknown biological threats. Combat should feel secondary to survival decisions.

# END OF FILE

# FILE : 01_WORLD\ISLANDS\SOUTH_BLUE\SB-008_WINDSONG_ISLE.md
# =====================================================

# ==================================================
# WINDSONG ISLE
# ==================================================

Sea: South Blue
Classification: Original Island
World Type: One Piece Parallel World

---

# OVERVIEW

Windsong Isle is a fast-moving trade and travel hub known for its constant strong winds that circle the island year-round. These winds allow ships to accelerate unusually fast, making the island a critical “speed corridor” for South Blue navigation.

It is also famous for its musical culture, where wind patterns are believed to carry “songs of the sea,” inspiring musicians, navigators, and pirates alike.

---

# GEOGRAPHY

Island Size

Medium

Approximate Diameter

90 km

Travel Time by Foot

2 Days

Highest Elevation

Aeolus Peak

Terrain Distribution

35% Wind Plains

25% Coastal Cliffs

15% Forest

15% Port Towns

10% High Altitude Ridges

---

# CLIMATE

Climate Type

Wind-Driven Oceanic

Conditions

- Constant directional winds
- Sudden gust storms
- Calm “eye zones” between wind cycles
- High sailing efficiency zones

---

# NATURAL RESOURCES

Primary Resources

- Lightweight Timber
- Windstone Minerals
- Sea Salt
- High-Speed Sailing Currents (natural phenomenon)

Rare Resources

- Aeolus Crystal
- Sonic Drift Ore
- Whisper Grass

Open plains where wind produces audible tones.

---

## Cyclone Channels

Dangerous wind corridors around the island.

|7|Ship modification offer appears.|
|8|Lost traveler rescued from cliffs.|
|9|Wind anomaly detected.|
|10|High-speed pirate ambush.|

---

# ADVENTURE HOOKS

- Track pirate fleet using wind routes.
- Investigate disappearing ships in calm zones.
- Study Aeolus Peak artifact rumor.
- Escort high-speed trade convoy.
- Uncover illegal ship modification network.
- Discover wind-based navigation secrets.
- Stop piracy using Cyclone Channels.

---

# HIDDEN LOCATIONS

## Aeolus Peak Core

Ancient structure influencing wind currents.

---

## Silent Wind Zone

Area where all sound disappears.

---

## Cliffside Ship Graveyard

---

## Hidden Wind Tunnel Network

Natural tunnels accelerating wind flow.

---

# SECRETS

Windsong Isle’s wind system may be partially artificial, created by unknown ancient engineering.

Some navigators can “hear” future weather patterns through wind resonance.

A forgotten Marine experiment attempted to control entire wind corridors.

---

# GM NOTES

Recommended Campaign Length

2–4 Sessions

Recommended Player Level

Intermediate

Core Themes

- Speed
- Navigation
- Music
- Maritime Strategy
- Chases

## GM Guidance

This island is ideal for chase arcs, high-speed naval combat, and navigation-focused storytelling. Encounters should feel dynamic, fast, and reactive.

# =====================================================
# FILE : 01_WORLD\ISLANDS\SOUTH_BLUE\SB-009_SANDGLASS_KEY.md
# =====================================================

# ==================================================
# SANDGLASS KEY
# ==================================================

Sea: South Blue
Classification: Ancient Mystery Island
World Type: One Piece Parallel World

---

# OVERVIEW

Sandglass Key is a legendary and heavily restricted island located at the edge of South Blue’s mapped territory.

It is known for its abnormal time-related phenomena, shifting landscapes, and ancient ruins that appear to predate recorded history of the seas.

Most navigation charts label this island as:

> “DO NOT APPROACH – STATIC SPACE ANOMALY”

Only a few Marine expedition logs confirm its existence.

---

# GEOGRAPHY

Island Size

Small–Medium

Approximate Diameter

70 km

Travel Time by Foot

1–2 Days (but inconsistent due to spatial distortion)

Highest Elevation

Hourglass Spire

Terrain Distribution

30% Desert Sand Zones

25% Ruins

20% Temporal Distortion Fields

15% Coastal Cliffs

10% Submerged Structures

---

# CLIMATE

Climate Type

Temporal Anomaly Zone

Conditions

- Time distortion zones
- Sudden weather shifts
- Sandstorms that reverse direction
- Day/night cycles vary unpredictably

---

# NATURAL RESOURCES

Primary Resources

- Chrono Sand (time-reactive sand)
- Ancient Stone
- Fossilized Coral
- Distortion Minerals

Rare Resources

- Hourglass Crystal
- Memory Resin
- Time-locked Shells

---

# MAJOR REGIONS

## Central Hourglass Ruins

Ancient city core shaped like a giant hourglass.

---

## Shifting Dunes

Desert region that changes layout over time.

---

## Echo Coast

Coastline where sound repeats after delays.

---

## Sunken Memory Trench

---

## Temporal Rift Fields

Zones where physics behaves unpredictably.

---
